1→import Foundation
     2→import Combine
     3→
     4→// MARK: - ChunkAssemblyManager
     5→
     6→/// Manages the collection and assembly of recording chunks received from Apple Watch.
     7→///
     8→/// When the Watch sends a chunked recording (45-minute segments), this manager:
     9→/// 1. Tracks received chunks per parent recording ID
    10→/// 2. Stores chunk file URLs and metadata
    11→/// 3. Detects when all chunks have been received
    12→/// 4. Triggers merge via AudioChunkMerger
    13→/// 5. Persists chunk inventory to disk (survives app kill)
    14→///
    15→/// For single-file (non-chunked) transfers, this manager is bypassed and the
    16→/// existing WatchRecordingService flow handles reception directly.
    17→///
    18→/// - Important: Chunk files are retained locally until the entire recording
    19→///   is assembled, uploaded, and confirmed to the Watch.
    20→@MainActor
    21→final class ChunkAssemblyManager: ObservableObject {
    22→    
    23→    // MARK: - Singleton
    24→    
    25→    static let shared = ChunkAssemblyManager()
    26→    
    27→    // MARK: - Types
    28→    
    29→    /// State of a recording being assembled from chunks
    30→    enum AssemblyState: Equatable {
    31→        /// Receiving chunks (not all arrived yet)
    32→        case receiving(chunksReceived: Int, totalExpected: Int?)
    33→        /// All chunks received, merging in progress
    34→        case merging
    35→        /// Merge complete, ready for upload
    36→        case ready(mergedFileURL: URL)
    37→        /// Assembly failed
    38→        case failed(String)
    39→        
    40→        static func == (lhs: AssemblyState, rhs: AssemblyState) -> Bool {
    41→            switch (lhs, rhs) {
    42→            case (.receiving(let lc, let lt), .receiving(let rc, let rt)):
    43→                return lc == rc && lt == rt
    44→            case (.merging, .merging):
    45→                return true
    46→            case (.ready(let lURL), .ready(let rURL)):
    47→                return lURL == rURL
    48→            case (.failed(let lMsg), .failed(let rMsg)):
    49→                return lMsg == rMsg
    50→            default:
    51→                return false
    52→            }
    53→        }
    54→    }
    55→    
    56→    /// Information about a recording being assembled
    57→    struct AssemblyInfo: Identifiable {
    58→        let id: UUID  // parentRecordingId
    59→        var chunks: [Int: URL]  // chunkIndex -> fileURL
    60→        var totalExpectedChunks: Int?  // Set when isLastChunk arrives
    61→        var totalDuration: TimeInterval  // Accumulated duration
    62→        var recordingStartTime: Date?
    63→        var state: AssemblyState
    64→        
    65→        /// True when all expected chunks have been received
    66→        var isComplete: Bool {
    67→            guard let expected = totalExpectedChunks else { return false }
    68→            return chunks.count == expected
    69→        }
    70→        
    71→        /// Returns chunk URLs sorted by index
    72→        var sortedChunkURLs: [URL] {
    73→            return (0..<(totalExpectedChunks ?? chunks.count))
    74→                .compactMap { chunks[$0] }
    75→        }
    76→    }
    77→    
    78→    // MARK: - Published Properties
    79→    
    80→    /// Active assemblies keyed by parent recording ID
    81→    @Published private(set) var activeAssemblies: [UUID: AssemblyInfo] = [:]
    82→    
    83→    // MARK: - Callbacks
    84→    
    85→    /// Called when a recording is fully assembled and ready for upload.
    86→    /// Parameters: parentRecordingId, mergedFileURL, totalDuration, recordingStartTime
    87→    var onRecordingAssembled: ((UUID, URL, TimeInterval, Date?) -> Void)?
    88→    
    89→    // MARK: - Private Properties
    90→    
    91→    private let logger = LoggerFactory.makeLogger()
    92→    
    93→    /// Core Data repository for persisting transfer lifecycle state.
    94→    /// Injected via `setWatchTransferRepository(_:)` after DI container is ready.
    95→    private var watchTransferRepository: WatchTransferRepository?
    96→    
    97→    /// Directory for storing received chunk files
    98→    private let chunksBaseDirectory: URL = {
    99→        let dir = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory
   100→            .appendingPathComponent("WatchRecordings", isDirectory: true)
   101→            .appendingPathComponent("chunks", isDirectory: true)
   102→        try? FileManager.default.createDirectory(at: dir, withIntermediateDirectories: true)
   103→        return dir
   104→    }()
   105→    
   106→    /// Persistent inventory file for surviving app kills
   107→    private var inventoryFileURL: URL {
   108→        return chunksBaseDirectory.appendingPathComponent("chunk_inventory.json")
   109→    }
   110→    
   111→    // MARK: - Initialization
   112→    
   113→    private init() {
   114→        logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Initialized")
   115→        // Restore any persisted chunk inventory from disk
   116→        restoreInventoryFromDisk()
   117→    }
   118→    
   119→    // MARK: - Configuration
   120→    
   121→    /// Sets the Core Data repository for persisting transfer state.
   122→    /// Called by WatchRecordingService.configure() after DI is ready.
   123→    func setWatchTransferRepository(_ repository: WatchTransferRepository) {
   124→        self.watchTransferRepository = repository
   125→    }
   126→    
   127→    /// Triggers merge for any assemblies that were complete before the app was killed.
   128→    /// MUST be called after `onRecordingAssembled` callback is set, otherwise the
   129→    /// merged file would be produced but never handed off to the upload pipeline.
   130→    func processRestoredAssemblies() {
   131→        let completeAssemblies = activeAssemblies.filter { $0.value.isComplete }
   132→        guard !completeAssemblies.isEmpty else { return }
   133→        
   134→        logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Processing \(completeAssemblies.count) restored complete assembly(ies)")
   135→        for (parentId, _) in completeAssemblies {
   136→            triggerMerge(parentId: parentId)
   137→        }
   138→    }
   139→    
   140→    // MARK: - Public Methods
   141→    
   142→    /// Handles a received chunk file from the Watch.
   143→    ///
   144→    /// Stores the chunk, updates assembly tracking, and triggers merge
   145→    /// if all chunks for the recording have been received.
   146→    ///
   147→    /// - Parameters:
   148→    ///   - fileURL: The local URL of the received chunk file (already copied to safe location)
   149→    ///   - metadata: The transfer metadata containing chunk information
   150→    func chunkReceived(fileURL: URL, metadata: RecordingTransferMetadata) {
   151→        guard let chunkInfo = metadata.chunkInfo else {
   152→            logger.error("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Received non-chunked file -- should not be routed here", error: nil)
   153→            return
   154→        }
   155→        
   156→        let parentId = chunkInfo.parentRecordingId
   157→        let chunkIndex = chunkInfo.chunkIndex
   158→        
   159→        logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Received chunk \(chunkIndex) for recording \(parentId)")
   160→        logger.debug("[ChunkAssemblyManager] isLastChunk: \(chunkInfo.isLastChunk), duration: \(chunkInfo.chunkDuration)s")
   161→        
   162→        // Move chunk file to organized directory
   163→        let recordingDir = chunksBaseDirectory.appendingPathComponent(parentId.uuidString, isDirectory: true)
   164→        try? FileManager.default.createDirectory(at: recordingDir, withIntermediateDirectories: true)
   165→        
   166→        let destinationURL = recordingDir.appendingPathComponent("chunk_\(chunkIndex).m4a")
   167→        
   168→        do {
   169→            let sourcePath = fileURL.standardizedFileURL.path
   170→            let destPath = destinationURL.standardizedFileURL.path
   171→            
   172→            if sourcePath == destPath {
   173→                // WatchConnectivityManager already placed the file at our destination path.
   174→                // The file is already where we need it -- no move required.
   175→                logger.debug("[ChunkAssemblyManager] File already at destination (placed by WCM) -- skipping move")
   176→            } else {
   177→                // Remove existing file if present (retry scenario)
   178→                if FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: destinationURL.path) {
   179→                    try FileManager.default.removeItem(at: destinationURL)
   180→                }
   181→                try FileManager.default.moveItem(at: fileURL, to: destinationURL)
   182→            }
   183→            
   184→            logger.debug("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Chunk stored at: \(destinationURL.path)")
   185→        } catch {
   186→            logger.error("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Failed to store chunk file", error: error)
   187→            updateAssemblyState(parentId: parentId, state: .failed("Failed to store chunk: \(error.localizedDescription)"))
   188→            return
   189→        }
   190→        
   191→        // Update or create assembly info
   192→        var assembly = activeAssemblies[parentId] ?? AssemblyInfo(
   193→            id: parentId,
   194→            chunks: [:],
   195→            totalExpectedChunks: nil,
   196→            totalDuration: 0,
   197→            recordingStartTime: chunkInfo.recordingStartTime,
   198→            state: .receiving(chunksReceived: 0, totalExpected: nil)
   199→        )
   200→        
   201→        // Store chunk reference
   202→        assembly.chunks[chunkIndex] = destinationURL
   203→        assembly.totalDuration = chunkInfo.totalDurationSoFar
   204→        assembly.recordingStartTime = chunkInfo.recordingStartTime
   205→        
   206→        // If this is the last chunk, we now know the total count
   207→        if chunkInfo.isLastChunk {
   208→            assembly.totalExpectedChunks = chunkIndex + 1
   209→            logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Last chunk received. Total expected: \(chunkIndex + 1)")
   210→        }
   211→        
   212→        // Update state
   213→        assembly.state = .receiving(
   214→            chunksReceived: assembly.chunks.count,
   215→            totalExpected: assembly.totalExpectedChunks
   216→        )
   217→        
   218→        activeAssemblies[parentId] = assembly
   219→        
   220→        // Persist inventory to disk
   221→        persistInventoryToDisk()
   222→        
   223→        // Check if all chunks are present
   224→        if assembly.isComplete {
   225→            logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] All \(assembly.chunks.count) chunks received for recording \(parentId)")
   226→            triggerMerge(parentId: parentId)
   227→        } else {
   228→            let received = assembly.chunks.count
   229→            let expected = assembly.totalExpectedChunks.map { String($0) } ?? "?"
   230→            logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Chunk \(chunkIndex) stored. Progress: \(received)/\(expected)")
   231→        }
   232→    }
   233→    
   234→    /// Returns assembly state for a given recording, or nil if not being assembled
   235→    func getAssemblyState(for parentRecordingId: UUID) -> AssemblyState? {
   236→        return activeAssemblies[parentRecordingId]?.state
   237→    }
   238→    
   239→    /// Cleans up assembly data for a recording after successful upload.
   240→    /// - Parameter parentRecordingId: The parent recording ID to clean up
   241→    func cleanupAssembly(for parentRecordingId: UUID) {
   242→        guard let assembly = activeAssemblies[parentRecordingId] else { return }
   243→        
   244→        // Delete chunk files
   245→        for (_, url) in assembly.chunks {
   246→            try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at: url)
   247→        }
   248→        
   249→        // Delete recording directory
   250→        let recordingDir = chunksBaseDirectory.appendingPathComponent(parentRecordingId.uuidString, isDirectory: true)
   251→        try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at: recordingDir)
   252→        
   253→        // Remove from tracking
   254→        activeAssemblies.removeValue(forKey: parentRecordingId)
   255→        persistInventoryToDisk()
   256→        
   257→        logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Cleaned up assembly for recording: \(parentRecordingId)")
   258→    }
   259→    
   260→    // MARK: - Private Methods
   261→    
   262→    /// Triggers the merge process for a complete recording.
   263→    /// After successful merge, individual chunk files are deleted (only the merged file is kept).
   264→    private func triggerMerge(parentId: UUID) {
   265→        guard var assembly = activeAssemblies[parentId], assembly.isComplete else { return }
   266→        
   267→        assembly.state = .merging
   268→        activeAssemblies[parentId] = assembly
   269→        
   270→        // Persist assembling status in Core Data
   271→        if let repo = watchTransferRepository {
   272→            try? repo.markAssembling(parentId)
   273→        }
   274→        
   275→        let sortedURLs = assembly.sortedChunkURLs
   276→        let totalDuration = assembly.totalDuration
   277→        let startTime = assembly.recordingStartTime
   278→        
   279→        logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Starting merge for recording \(parentId) (\(sortedURLs.count) chunks)")
   280→        
   281→        Task {
   282→            do {
   283→                let mergedURL = try await AudioChunkMerger.merge(
   284→                    chunks: sortedURLs,
   285→                    outputFilename: "recording_\(parentId.uuidString).m4a"
   286→                )
   287→                
   288→                logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Merge complete: \(mergedURL.path)")
   289→                
   290→                // Delete individual chunk files now that we have the merged output
   291→                deleteChunkFiles(for: parentId)
   292→                
   293→                // Update state
   294→                var updatedAssembly = activeAssemblies[parentId]
   295→                updatedAssembly?.state = .ready(mergedFileURL: mergedURL)
   296→                if let updated = updatedAssembly {
   297→                    activeAssemblies[parentId] = updated
   298→                }
   299→                persistInventoryToDisk()
   300→                
   301→                // Persist assembled status in Core Data
   302→                if let repo = watchTransferRepository {
   303→                    try? repo.markAssembled(parentId)
   304→                }
   305→                
   306→                // Notify that recording is ready for upload
   307→                onRecordingAssembled?(parentId, mergedURL, totalDuration, startTime)
   308→                
   309→            } catch {
   310→                logger.error("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Merge failed for recording \(parentId)", error: error)
   311→                updateAssemblyState(parentId: parentId, state: .failed("Merge failed: \(error.localizedDescription)"))
   312→                
   313→                // Persist failure in Core Data and increment retry counter
   314→                if let repo = watchTransferRepository {
   315→                    try? repo.markFailed(parentId, error: "Merge failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   316→                    try? repo.incrementMergeRetryCount(parentId)
   317→                }
   318→            }
   319→        }
   320→    }
   321→    
   322→    /// Deletes individual chunk files for a recording while keeping the merged output file.
   323→    /// Called after a successful merge to free disk space.
   324→    private func deleteChunkFiles(for parentId: UUID) {
   325→        guard let assembly = activeAssemblies[parentId] else { return }
   326→        
   327→        var deletedCount = 0
   328→        for (index, url) in assembly.chunks {
   329→            do {
   330→                try FileManager.default.removeItem(at: url)
   331→                deletedCount += 1
   332→            } catch {
   333→                logger.warning("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Could not delete chunk \(index): \(error.localizedDescription)")
   334→            }
   335→        }
   336→        
   337→        // Delete the recording's chunk directory (will succeed if all files were removed)
   338→        let recordingDir = chunksBaseDirectory.appendingPathComponent(parentId.uuidString, isDirectory: true)
   339→        try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at: recordingDir)
   340→        
   341→        logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Deleted \(deletedCount)/\(assembly.chunks.count) chunk files for recording \(parentId)")
   342→    }
   343→    
   344→    /// Updates the assembly state for a recording
   345→    private func updateAssemblyState(parentId: UUID, state: AssemblyState) {
   346→        if var assembly = activeAssemblies[parentId] {
   347→            assembly.state = state
   348→            activeAssemblies[parentId] = assembly
   349→        }
   350→    }
   351→    
   352→    // MARK: - Merge Retry
   353→    
   354→    /// Retries merge for transfers that previously failed, up to `maxRetries` attempts.
   355→    /// Called on app launch to recover from transient merge errors.
   356→    func retryFailedMerges(maxRetries: Int = 3) {
   357→        guard let repo = watchTransferRepository else { return }
   358→        
   359→        guard let retryable = try? repo.fetchRetryableMerges(maxRetries: maxRetries), !retryable.isEmpty else { return }
   360→        
   361→        logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Found \(retryable.count) failed merge(s) eligible for retry")
   362→        
   363→        for transfer in retryable {
   364→            guard let recordingId = transfer.id else { continue }
   365→            
   366→            // The assembly must exist in-memory (restored from disk) for merge to work
   367→            guard let assembly = activeAssemblies[recordingId], assembly.isComplete else {
   368→                logger.warning("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Skipping retry for \(recordingId) — assembly not in memory or incomplete")
   369→                continue
   370→            }
   371→            
   372→            logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Retrying merge for \(recordingId) (attempt \(transfer.mergeRetryCount + 1))")
   373→            triggerMerge(parentId: recordingId)
   374→        }
   375→    }
   376→    
   377→    // MARK: - Persistence
   378→    
   379→    /// Persists chunk inventory to disk so it survives app kills.
   380→    /// Only stores minimal data (file paths and chunk indices), not file contents.
   381→    private func persistInventoryToDisk() {
   382→        let inventory = activeAssemblies.mapValues { assembly -> [String: Any] in
   383→            var dict: [String: Any] = [
   384→                "chunks": Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: assembly.chunks.map { (String($0.key), $0.value.path) }),
   385→                "totalDuration": assembly.totalDuration
   386→            ]
   387→            if let expected = assembly.totalExpectedChunks {
   388→                dict["totalExpectedChunks"] = expected
   389→            }
   390→            if let startTime = assembly.recordingStartTime {
   391→                dict["recordingStartTime"] = startTime.timeIntervalSince1970
   392→            }
   393→            return dict
   394→        }
   395→        
   396→        let keyed = inventory.reduce(into: [String: Any]()) { result, pair in
   397→            result[pair.key.uuidString] = pair.value
   398→        }
   399→        
   400→        do {
   401→            let data = try JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: keyed, options: .prettyPrinted)
   402→            try data.write(to: inventoryFileURL, options: .atomic)
   403→            logger.debug("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Inventory persisted to disk")
   404→        } catch {
   405→            logger.error("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Failed to persist inventory", error: error)
   406→        }
   407→    }
   408→    
   409→    /// Restores chunk inventory from disk after app relaunch.
   410→    private func restoreInventoryFromDisk() {
   411→        guard FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: inventoryFileURL.path) else { return }
   412→        
   413→        do {
   414→            let data = try Data(contentsOf: inventoryFileURL)
   415→            guard let keyed = try JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: data) as? [String: [String: Any]] else { return }
   416→            
   417→            var restored: [UUID: AssemblyInfo] = [:]
   418→            
   419→            for (idString, dict) in keyed {
   420→                guard let parentId = UUID(uuidString: idString),
   421→                      let chunksDict = dict["chunks"] as? [String: String] else { continue }
   422→                
   423→                let totalDuration = dict["totalDuration"] as? TimeInterval ?? 0
   424→                let expectedChunks = dict["totalExpectedChunks"] as? Int
   425→                let startTimestamp = dict["recordingStartTime"] as? TimeInterval
   426→                let startTime = startTimestamp.map { Date(timeIntervalSince1970: $0) }
   427→                
   428→                // Reconstruct chunks dictionary, verifying files still exist
   429→                var validChunks: [Int: URL] = [:]
   430→                for (indexStr, pathStr) in chunksDict {
   431→                    if let index = Int(indexStr) {
   432→                        let url = URL(fileURLWithPath: pathStr)
   433→                        if FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: url.path) {
   434→                            validChunks[index] = url
   435→                        }
   436→                    }
   437→                }
   438→                
   439→                guard !validChunks.isEmpty else { continue }
   440→                
   441→                let assembly = AssemblyInfo(
   442→                    id: parentId,
   443→                    chunks: validChunks,
   444→                    totalExpectedChunks: expectedChunks,
   445→                    totalDuration: totalDuration,
   446→                    recordingStartTime: startTime,
   447→                    state: .receiving(chunksReceived: validChunks.count, totalExpected: expectedChunks)
   448→                )
   449→                
   450→                restored[parentId] = assembly
   451→                
   452→                // If assembly was already complete before app kill, trigger merge
   453→                if assembly.isComplete {
   454→                    logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Restored complete assembly for \(parentId) -- triggering merge")
   455→                }
   456→            }
   457→            
   458→            activeAssemblies = restored
   459→            
   460→            if !restored.isEmpty {
   461→                logger.info("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Restored \(restored.count) assembly(ies) from disk")
   462→                // NOTE: Do NOT trigger merge here. The onRecordingAssembled callback
   463→                // hasn't been set yet (set by WatchRecordingService.configure).
   464→                // Call processRestoredAssemblies() after the callback is wired up.
   465→            }
   466→            
   467→        } catch {
   468→            logger.error("[ChunkAssemblyManager] Failed to restore inventory from disk", error: error)
   469→        }
   470→    }
   471→}
   472→

content.content:
1→//
     2→//  WatchConnectivityManager.swift
     3→//  Memorable
     4→//
     5→//  Created by Anshuman Sharma on 23/12/25.
     6→//
     7→
     8→import Foundation
     9→import WatchConnectivity
    10→import Combine
    11→
    12→final class WatchConnectivityManager: NSObject, ObservableObject {
    13→
    14→    static let shared = WatchConnectivityManager()
    15→    
    16→    /// Logger instance for connectivity-related logging
    17→    private let logger = LoggerFactory.makeLogger()
    18→
    19→    @Published private(set) var isSupported: Bool = WCSession.isSupported()
    20→    @Published private(set) var activationState: WCSessionActivationState = .notActivated
    21→    @Published public private(set) var isReachable: Bool = false
    22→    @Published private(set) var receivedMessage: [String: Any]? = nil
    23→    @Published private(set) var lastContext: [String: Any]? = nil
    24→    @Published private(set) var lastError: Error? = nil
    25→    
    26→    /// Clears the last error. Call this when operations succeed to prevent stale error state.
    27→    func clearLastError() {
    28→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
    29→            self.lastError = nil
    30→        }
    31→    }
    32→    @Published public private(set) var isWatchConnected: Bool = false
    33→    @Published public private(set) var isPaired: Bool = false
    34→    @Published public private(set) var isWatchAppInstalled: Bool = false
    35→    
    36→    // Recording transfer state tracking - per-recording (no global state to support concurrent transfers)
    37→    @Published public private(set) var lastTransferredRecordingId: UUID? = nil
    38→    
    39→    // MARK: - Constants
    40→    
    41→    /// Maximum progress increment per KVO update (5%) to smooth out progress jumps
    42→    /// Prevents jarring UI updates when progress jumps forward rapidly
    43→    private static let maxProgressIncrement: Double = 0.05
    44→    
    45→    /// Delay in seconds before auto-removing completed transfers from UI
    46→    /// Gives users time to see the completion state before it disappears
    47→    private static let completedTransferAutoRemoveDelay: TimeInterval = 3.0
    48→    
    49→    #if os(watchOS)
    50→    // Transfer progress per recording ID (0.0 to 1.0)
    51→    // CRITICAL: @Published properties must be accessed on main thread
    52→    // All mutations happen on main thread via DispatchQueue.main.async
    53→    @Published private(set) var recordingTransferProgress: [UUID: Double] = [:]
    54→    
    55→    /// Cached aggregate transfer state (updated when progress changes)
    56→    /// Avoids deadlock risk from DispatchQueue.main.sync
    57→    @Published public private(set) var cachedAggregateTransferState: RecordingTransferState = .idle
    58→    
    59→    /// Internal helper to compute aggregate state (must be called on main thread)
    60→    private func computeAggregateState() -> RecordingTransferState {
    61→        assert(Thread.isMainThread, "computeAggregateState must be called on main thread")
    62→        
    63→        if recordingTransferProgress.isEmpty {
    64→            return .idle
    65→        }
    66→        
    67→        // Find max progress and any failures
    68→        let progressValues = recordingTransferProgress.values
    69→        if let maxProgress = progressValues.max() {
    70→            if maxProgress >= 1.0 && progressValues.allSatisfy({ $0 >= 1.0 }) {
    71→                return .completed
    72→            }
    73→            return .transferring(progress: maxProgress)
    74→        }
    75→        
    76→        return .idle
    77→    }
    78→    #endif
    79→    
    80→    #if os(iOS)
    81→    /// Whether the Watch has granted microphone permission (synced via application context)
    82→    /// `nil` means we haven't received the status from the Watch yet
    83→    @Published public private(set) var watchMicrophonePermissionGranted: Bool? = nil
    84→    
    85→    /// True when the Watch has started recording and has not yet stopped (iPhone shows Live Activity / banner).
    86→    @Published public private(set) var isRecordingOnWatch: Bool = false
    87→    
    88→    /// When recording started on Watch (for elapsed time in Live Activity). Nil when not recording.
    89→    @Published public private(set) var recordingStartedAt: Date? = nil
    90→    
    91→    /// True when `isRecordingOnWatch` was set from cached activation context (potentially stale).
    92→    /// Observers should treat this as unconfirmed and avoid side-effects like notifications
    93→    /// until fresh context arrives (at which point this becomes `false`).
    94→    @Published public private(set) var isRecordingStateFromActivation: Bool = false
    95→    
    96→    /// Sentinel UUID for placeholder pending transfers (never maps to real recordings)
    97→    /// Public so WatchRecordingService can filter it out from UI
    98→    public static let pendingPlaceholderID = UUID(uuidString: "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001")!
    99→    
   100→    /// State tracking for incoming transfers from watch (per-recording)
   101→    /// Tracks state transitions and progress for real-time updates
   102→    public enum IncomingTransferState: Equatable {
   103→        case pending          // Transfer announced but not started (hasContentPending = true)
   104→        case receiving(progress: Double?)  // File is being received (with optional progress 0.0-1.0)
   105→        case completed        // Transfer complete and saved
   106→        case failed(String)   // Transfer failed with error message
   107→        
   108→        public static func == (lhs: IncomingTransferState, rhs: IncomingTransferState) -> Bool {
   109→            switch (lhs, rhs) {
   110→            case (.pending, .pending), (.completed, .completed):
   111→                return true
   112→            case (.receiving(let lhsProgress), .receiving(let rhsProgress)):
   113→                // Compare progress values (with small tolerance for floating point)
   114→                if let lhs = lhsProgress, let rhs = rhsProgress {
   115→                    return abs(lhs - rhs) < 0.001
   116→                }
   117→                return lhsProgress == nil && rhsProgress == nil
   118→            case (.failed(let lhsError), .failed(let rhsError)):
   119→                return lhsError == rhsError
   120→            default:
   121→                return false
   122→            }
   123→        }
   124→    }
   125→    
   126→    // Track incoming transfers from watch (per-recording progress state)
   127→    // CRITICAL: @Published properties must be accessed on main thread
   128→    @Published public private(set) var incomingTransferProgress: [UUID: IncomingTransferState] = [:]
   129→    
   130→    /// Last timestamp when incoming progress changed for a recording.
   131→    /// Used to detect stale "stuck at X%" progress entries coming from cached context.
   132→    private var incomingProgressLastChangeAt: [UUID: Date] = [:]
   133→    
   134→    /// Last known incoming progress value per recording.
   135→    private var incomingProgressLastValue: [UUID: Double] = [:]
   136→    
   137→    /// Recordings whose stale progress has been timed out and should not be re-added
   138→    /// from repeated cached context updates unless progress meaningfully changes.
   139→    private var suppressedStaleIncomingProgressIds: Set<UUID> = []
   140→    
   141→    /// Shared timeout policy (3 minutes per 30 minutes of recording duration).
   142→    private let transferTimeoutPolicy = TransferTimeoutPolicy.default
   143→    
   144→    /// Cached aggregate state of all incoming transfers (updated when state changes)
   145→    /// Avoids deadlock risk from DispatchQueue.main.sync
   146→    @Published public private(set) var cachedAggregateIncomingTransferState: RecordingTransferState = .idle
   147→    
   148→    /// Internal helper to compute aggregate incoming transfer state (must be called on main thread)
   149→    private func computeAggregateIncomingState() -> RecordingTransferState {
   150→        assert(Thread.isMainThread, "computeAggregateIncomingState must be called on main thread")
   151→        
   152→        // Filter out placeholder UUID (sentinel value)
   153→        let realTransfers = incomingTransferProgress.filter { $0.key != Self.pendingPlaceholderID }
   154→        
   155→        if realTransfers.isEmpty {
   156→            return .idle
   157→        }
   158→        
   159→        // Check for any failures
   160→        for state in realTransfers.values {
   161→            if case .failed(let error) = state {
   162→                return .failed(error)
   163→            }
   164→        }
   165→        
   166→        // Check if all are completed
   167→        let allCompleted = realTransfers.values.allSatisfy { state in
   168→            if case .completed = state {
   169→                return true
   170→            }
   171→            return false
   172→        }
   173→        
   174→        if allCompleted {
   175→            return .completed
   176→        }
   177→        
   178→        // Find max progress from receiving transfers
   179→        var maxProgress: Double = 0.0
   180→        var hasActive = false
   181→        
   182→        for state in realTransfers.values {
   183→            if case .pending = state {
   184→                hasActive = true
   185→            } else if case .receiving(let progress) = state {
   186→                hasActive = true
   187→                if let progress = progress {
   188→                    maxProgress = max(maxProgress, progress)
   189→                }
   190→            }
   191→        }
   192→        
   193→        if hasActive {
   194→            // Use actual progress if available, otherwise use 0.5 as placeholder
   195→            let displayProgress = maxProgress > 0 ? maxProgress : 0.5
   196→            return .transferring(progress: displayProgress)
   197→        }
   198→        
   199→        return .idle
   200→    }

1→//
     2→//  WatchRecordingService.swift
     3→//  Memorable
     4→//
     5→//  Handles reception and storage of recordings transferred from Apple Watch.
     6→//  This service sets up the WatchConnectivityManager callbacks to receive
     7→//  recording files, save them to local storage, and automatically submit
     8→//  them for transcription.
     9→//
    10→
    11→import Foundation
    12→import Combine
    13→import WatchConnectivity
    14→#if os(iOS)
    15→import UIKit
    16→#endif
    17→
    18→/// Service responsible for handling recordings received from Apple Watch.
    19→/// Automatically submits received recordings to the transcription service.
    20→/// Should be initialized early in the app lifecycle to ensure recordings are captured.
    21→///
    22→/// Flow:
    23→/// 1. Watch records audio → transfers to iOS
    24→/// 2. WatchRecordingService receives file via WatchConnectivityManager
    25→/// 3. File is saved to local storage
    26→/// 4. Recording is automatically submitted for transcription
    27→/// 5. Confirmation sent back to watch to delete local copy
    28→@MainActor
    29→final class WatchRecordingService: ObservableObject {
    30→    
    31→    // MARK: - Singleton
    32→    
    33→    static let shared = WatchRecordingService()
    34→    
    35→    // MARK: - Published Properties
    36→    
    37→    /// Number of recordings received from watch in current session
    38→    @Published private(set) var recordingsReceivedCount: Int = 0
    39→    
    40→    /// Last received recording (if any)
    41→    @Published private(set) var lastReceivedRecording: Recording? = nil
    42→    
    43→    /// Indicates if the service is currently processing a received file
    44→    @Published private(set) var isProcessingTransfer: Bool = false
    45→    
    46→    /// Indicates if there are pending files from when app was terminated
    47→    @Published private(set) var hasPendingFiles: Bool = false
    48→    
    49→    /// Information about incoming transfers from watch (per-recording)
    50→    @Published private(set) var incomingTransfers: [UUID: IncomingTransferInfo] = [:]
    51→    
    52→    // MARK: - Incoming Transfer Info
    53→    
    54→    /// Information about an incoming transfer from watch
    55→    struct IncomingTransferInfo: Identifiable {
    56→        let id: UUID
    57→        let metadata: RecordingTransferMetadata?
    58→        var state: WatchConnectivityManager.IncomingTransferState
    59→        var progress: Double?  // Optional - can be nil for indeterminate progress
    60→        var uploadId: UUID?  // Track associated upload
    61→        var uploadProgress: Double?  // Upload progress (0.0 to 1.0)
    62→        
    63→        // MARK: - Chunk-Aware Fields
    64→        
    65→        /// Number of chunks received so far (nil for single-file transfers)
    66→        var chunksReceived: Int?
    67→        
    68→        /// Total expected chunks (nil if last chunk not yet received, or single-file)
    69→        var totalExpectedChunks: Int?
    70→        
    71→        /// True if the recording is currently being merged from chunks
    72→        var isMerging: Bool = false
    73→        
    74→        /// Last time progress was updated (for stalled transfer detection)
    75→        var lastProgressUpdateTime: Date?
    76→        
    77→        // MARK: - Computed Properties
    78→        
    79→        var isUploading: Bool {
    80→            return uploadId != nil && uploadProgress != nil && uploadProgress! < 1.0
    81→        }
    82→        
    83→        /// True if this transfer appears stalled (no progress update in 2+ minutes).
    84→        /// Shown as a visual warning in the UI before the 5-minute hard timeout kicks in.
    85→        var isStalled: Bool {
    86→            guard let lastUpdate = lastProgressUpdateTime else { return false }
    87→            return Date().timeIntervalSince(lastUpdate) > 120
    88→        }
    89→        
    90→        /// Human-readable chunk progress (e.g., "2 of 3 parts received")
    91→        var chunkProgressText: String? {
    92→            guard let received = chunksReceived else { return nil }
    93→            if let total = totalExpectedChunks {
    94→                return "\(received) of \(total) parts received"
    95→            } else {
    96→                return "\(received) part(s) received"
    97→            }
    98→        }
    99→        
   100→        /// Total recording duration from chunk metadata (shows accumulated duration, not per-chunk)
   101→        var totalRecordingDuration: TimeInterval? {
   102→            return metadata?.chunkInfo?.totalDurationSoFar ?? metadata?.duration
   103→        }
   104→        
   105→        init(id: UUID, metadata: RecordingTransferMetadata? = nil, state: WatchConnectivityManager.IncomingTransferState, uploadId: UUID? = nil, uploadProgress: Double? = nil, lastProgressUpdateTime: Date = Date()) {
   106→            self.id = id
   107→            self.metadata = metadata
   108→            self.state = state
   109→            self.uploadId = uploadId
   110→            self.uploadProgress = uploadProgress
   111→            self.lastProgressUpdateTime = lastProgressUpdateTime
   112→            // Extract progress from state
   113→            switch state {
   114→            case .pending:
   115→                self.progress = nil // Indeterminate
   116→            case .receiving(let progress):
   117→                self.progress = progress // Use progress from watch if available
   118→            case .completed:
   119→                self.progress = 1.0 // 100%
   120→            case .failed:
   121→                self.progress = nil // No progress on failure
   122→            }
   123→        }
   124→    }

   208→            #if os(watchOS)

   223→    // Cached application context (thread-safe, updated on main thread)
   224→    // Used instead of reading session.receivedApplicationContext directly from background threads
   225→    @Published private var cachedApplicationContext: [String: Any] = [:]
   226→    
   227→    /// Thread-safe access to cached application context
   228→    /// CRITICAL: Must be accessed from main thread only to avoid deadlocks
   229→    /// @Published properties are main-thread only, so this getter enforces that requirement
   230→    var applicationContext: [String: Any] {
   231→        dispatchPrecondition(condition: .onQueue(.main))
   232→        return cachedApplicationContext
   233→    }
   234→    
   235→    /// Safely retrieves cached application context, ensuring main thread access
   236→    /// This method should only be called from contexts where you can guarantee main thread execution
   237→    /// or where async dispatch is acceptable. For synchronous access from main thread, use applicationContext property directly.
   238→    /// - Returns: The cached application context, or lastContext if cached is empty
   239→    private func getCachedApplicationContextSafely() -> [String: Any] {
   240→        dispatchPrecondition(condition: .onQueue(.main))
   241→        let context = cachedApplicationContext
   242→        return context.isEmpty ? (lastContext ?? [:]) : context
   243→    }

   315→    private override init() {
   316→        guard WCSession.isSupported() else {
   317→            session = nil
   318→            super.init()
   319→            return
   320→        }
   321→
   322→        session = WCSession.default
   323→        super.init()
   324→
   325→        session?.delegate = self
   326→        session?.activate()
   327→        
   328→        // Initialize cached context if session is already activated
   329→        if let session = session, session.activationState == .activated {
   330→            DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   331→                self?.cachedApplicationContext = session.receivedApplicationContext
   332→                self?.lastContext = session.receivedApplicationContext
   333→            }
   334→        }
   335→    }
   336→    
   337→    deinit {
   338→        #if os(watchOS)
   339→        // Clean up all KVO observers
   340→        transferStateQueue.sync {
   341→            for observer in progressObservers.values {
   342→                observer.invalidate()
   343→            }
   344→            progressObservers.removeAll()
   345→            
   346→            for observer in chunkProgressObservers.values {
   347→                observer.invalidate()
   348→            }
   349→            chunkProgressObservers.removeAll()
   350→            chunkTransfers.removeAll()
   351→            recordingChunkCounts.removeAll()
   352→            recordingCompletedChunks.removeAll()
   353→            chunkProgressValues.removeAll()
   354→        }
   355→        #endif
   356→    }

   359→    private var hasLoggedInitialStatus: Bool = false
   360→    
   361→    /// Updates connection status from session properties.
   362→    /// NOTE: Must be called from main thread (already dispatched in call sites).
   363→    /// WCSession properties are accessed on main thread as per Apple's documentation.
   364→    private func updateConnectionStatus() {
   365→        dispatchPrecondition(condition: .onQueue(.main))
   366→        
   367→        guard let session = self.session else { return }
   368→            
   369→            // Read all session properties on main queue (they're UI-related)
   370→            #if os(iOS)
   371→            self.isPaired = session.isPaired
   372→            self.isWatchAppInstalled = session.isWatchAppInstalled
   373→            self.isWatchConnected = session.isPaired && session.isWatchAppInstalled
   374→        
   375→        // Check for pending content - files being transferred from watch
   376→        // Note: We can't get specific metadata until files arrive, so we show generic "pending" state
   377→        // Store placeholder UUID in a property to track it
   378→        let placeholderId = Self.pendingPlaceholderID
   379→        let hasRealTransfers = incomingTransferProgress.keys.contains { $0 != placeholderId }
   380→        
   381→        if session.hasContentPending {
   382→            // There are pending files but we don't know which recordings yet
   383→            // We'll update to specific states when files arrive in session(_:didReceive:)
   384→            // Check if we already have a pending placeholder
   385→            let hasPendingPlaceholder = incomingTransferProgress[placeholderId] != nil
   386→            
   387→            // Only add placeholder if:
   388→            // 1. We don't already have one
   389→            // 2. We have no real transfers (only add if truly empty)
   390→            if !hasPendingPlaceholder && !hasRealTransfers {
   391→                incomingTransferProgress[placeholderId] = .pending
   392→                updateCachedAggregateStates()
   393→                logger.debug("[WatchConnectivity] Added placeholder for pending content")
   394→            } else if hasPendingPlaceholder && hasRealTransfers {
   395→                // We have real transfers now, remove placeholder
   396→                incomingTransferProgress.removeValue(forKey: placeholderId)
   397→                updateCachedAggregateStates()
   398→                logger.debug("[WatchConnectivity] Removed placeholder - real transfers exist")
   399→            }

200→        setupIncomingTransferObserver()
   201→        setupUploadProgressObserver()
   202→        setupRecordingOnWatchObserver()
   203→        setupTransferCoordinationObservers()
   204→        setupStallDetectionTimer()
   205→        // Log Live Activities diagnostics
   206→        liveActivityManager.printDiagnostics()
   207→    
   208→    }
   209→    
   210→    /// Sets up observer for upload progress updates.
   211→    /// Throttled to avoid flooding ActivityKit with updates (it rate-limits at ~1/sec).
   212→    private func setupUploadProgressObserver() {
   213→        uploadProgressCancellable = UploadManager.shared.$activeUploads
   214→            .throttle(for: .seconds(1.0), scheduler: DispatchQueue.main, latest: true)
   215→            .receive(on: DispatchQueue.main)
   216→            .sink { [weak self] uploads in
   217→                guard let self = self else { return }
   218→                
   219→                
   220→                for (uploadId, uploadProgress) in uploads {
   221→                    log("   Upload \(uploadId): \(Int(uploadProgress.progress * 100))% - \(uploadProgress.status)")
   222→                    
   223→                    guard let recordingId = self.uploadIdToRecordingId[uploadId] else {
   224→                        log("   ⚠️ No recording mapped to upload ID: \(uploadId)")
   225→                        continue
   226→                    }
   227→                    
   228→                    log("   ✅ Linked to recording: \(recordingId)")
   229→                    
   230→                    if var transfer = self.incomingTransfers[recordingId] {
   231→                        transfer.uploadId = uploadId
   232→                        transfer.uploadProgress = uploadProgress.progress
   233→                        self.incomingTransfers[recordingId] = transfer
   234→                        log("   ✅ Updated transfer info with progress: \(Int(uploadProgress.progress * 100))%")
   235→                        
   236→                        self.updateLiveActivityForTransfer(
   237→                            recordingId: recordingId,
   238→                            transferInfo: transfer
   239→                        )
   240→                    } else {
   241→                        // Transfer was dropped (e.g., incoming transfer observer rebuilt the dict
   242→                        // before upload progress was set). Re-create so cloud upload stays visible.
   243→                        log("   ⚠️ Re-creating transfer for recording: \(recordingId)")
   244→                        let metadata = self.connectivityManager.getTransferMetadata(for: recordingId)
   245→                        let recreated = IncomingTransferInfo(
   246→                            id: recordingId,
   247→                            metadata: metadata,
   248→                            state: .completed,
   249→                            uploadId: uploadId,
   250→                            uploadProgress: uploadProgress.progress
   251→                        )
   252→                        self.incomingTransfers[recordingId] = recreated
   253→                        
   254→                        self.updateLiveActivityForTransfer(
   255→                            recordingId: recordingId,
   256→                            transferInfo: recreated
   257→                        )
   258→                        log("   ✅ Re-created transfer with upload progress: \(Int(uploadProgress.progress * 100))%")
   259→                    }
   260→                    
   261→                    // Sync upload progress to watch (throttle to every 10%)
   262→                    let progress = uploadProgress.progress
   263→                    let progressPercent = Int(progress * 100)
   264→                    let lastSent = self.lastSentUploadProgress[recordingId] ?? -1
   265→                    
   266→                    if progressPercent >= lastSent + 10 || progress >= 1.0 {
   267→                        WatchStatusSync().sendStatusToWatch(
   268→                            recordingId: recordingId,
   269→                            status: .uploading,
   270→                            uploadProgress: progress
   271→                        )
   272→                        self.lastSentUploadProgress[recordingId] = progressPercent
   273→                        log("   📤 Sent progress update to watch: \(progressPercent)%")
   274→                        
   275→                        if progress >= 1.0 {
   276→                            log("   ✅ Upload reached 100% for recording: \(recordingId)")
   277→                        }
   278→                    }
   279→                }
   280→            }
   281→    }

   283→    // MARK: - Configuration
   284→    
   285→    /// Configures dependencies for automatic upload and meeting creation.
   286→    /// Call this after AppContainer is set up.
   287→    /// - Parameters:
   288→    ///   - locationManager: The LocationManager for getting location data
   289→    ///   - createMeetingFromAudioUseCase: The use case for creating meetings from audio files
   290→    ///   - metadataRepository: The repository for recording metadata persistence
   291→    func configure(
   292→        locationManager: LocationManager,
   293→        createMeetingFromAudioUseCase: CreateMeetingFromAudioUseCase,
   294→        metadataRepository: RecordingMetadataRepository,
   295→        watchTransferRepository: WatchTransferRepository
   296→    ) {
   297→
   298→        self.locationManager = locationManager
   299→        self.createMeetingFromAudioUseCase = createMeetingFromAudioUseCase
   300→        self.metadataRepository = metadataRepository
   301→        self.watchTransferRepository = watchTransferRepository
   302→        
   303→        // Clear pre-configure fallback dedup cache once durable Core Data dedup is ready.
   304→        // Keeping these keys beyond startup can incorrectly block legitimate retries.
   305→        preConfigureDeliveryKeys.removeAll()
   306→        
   307→        // Share the repository with the chunk assembly manager
   308→        ChunkAssemblyManager.shared.setWatchTransferRepository(watchTransferRepository)
   309→        
   310→        // Configure chunk assembly manager to route merged recordings into the upload pipeline.
   311→        // IMPORTANT: callback must be set BEFORE processRestoredAssemblies() to avoid
   312→        // merged files being produced but never handed off to the upload pipeline.
   313→        setupChunkAssemblyCallback()
   314→        
   315→        // Now that the callback is wired, process any assemblies that were complete before app kill
   316→        ChunkAssemblyManager.shared.processRestoredAssemblies()
   317→        
   318→        // Clean up completed transfers older than 24 hours
   319→        try? watchTransferRepository.deleteCompleted(olderThan: Date().addingTimeInterval(-86400))
   320→    }
   321→    
   322→    // MARK: - Chunk Assembly Integration
   323→    
   324→    /// Sets up the callback for when ChunkAssemblyManager finishes merging a recording.
   325→    /// The merged file is treated like a single-file recording from this point forward:
   326→    /// saved to permanent storage, metadata updated, and uploaded.
   327→    private func setupChunkAssemblyCallback() {
   328→        ChunkAssemblyManager.shared.onRecordingAssembled = { [weak self] parentRecordingId, mergedFileURL, totalDuration, recordingStartTime in
   329→            guard let self = self else { return }
   330→            
   331→            self.logger.info("[WatchRecordingService] Assembled recording ready: \(parentRecordingId)")
   332→            self.logger.info("[WatchRecordingService] Merged file: \(mergedFileURL.path), duration: \(totalDuration)s")
   333→            
   334→            // Build metadata for the assembled recording
   335→            let assembledMetadata = RecordingTransferMetadata(
   336→                id: parentRecordingId,
   337→                filename: mergedFileURL.lastPathComponent,
   338→                duration: totalDuration,
   339→                createdAt: Date(),
   340→                startTime: recordingStartTime ?? Date()
   341→            )
   342→            
   343→            // Process like a single-file recording (save, upload, confirm)
   344→            self.handleAssembledRecording(fileURL: mergedFileURL, metadata: assembledMetadata)
   345→        }
   346→        
   347→        logger.info("[WatchRecordingService] ChunkAssemblyManager callback configured")
   348→    }
   349→    
   350→    /// Handles a fully assembled (merged) recording from the chunk pipeline.
   351→    /// This mirrors the single-file flow: save to permanent storage, upload, confirm.
   352→    /// - Parameters:
   353→    ///   - fileURL: The merged file URL
   354→    ///   - metadata: Metadata for the assembled recording
   355→    private func handleAssembledRecording(fileURL: URL, metadata: RecordingTransferMetadata) {
   356→        log("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════")
   357→        log("📥 ASSEMBLED RECORDING READY FOR UPLOAD")
   358→        log("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════")
   359→        log("Recording ID: \(metadata.id)")
   360→        log("Duration: \(String(format: "%.2f", metadata.duration))s")
   361→        
   362→        guard let repository = repository else {
   363→            logError("❌ Repository not initialized for assembled recording!", error: nil)
   364→            return
   365→        }
   366→        
   367→        do {
   368→            // Save to permanent storage (same as single-file flow)
   369→            let savedRecording = try repository.saveRecordingFromWatch(
   370→                sourceURL: fileURL,
   371→                id: metadata.id,
   372→                filename: metadata.filename,
   373→                duration: metadata.duration,
   374→                createdAt: metadata.createdAt,
   375→                startTime: metadata.startTime
   376→            )
   377→            
   378→            // Create/update metadata
   379→            if let metadataRepo = metadataRepository {
   380→                do {
   381→                    if try metadataRepo.fetchById(metadata.id) == nil {
   382→                        _ = try metadataRepo.create(recordingId: metadata.id, filePath: savedRecording.fileURL.path)
   383→                    }
   384→                    try metadataRepo.updateFilePath(metadata.id, filePath: savedRecording.fileURL.path)
   385→                    try metadataRepo.updateStatus(metadata.id, status: .received, error: nil)
   386→                    try? repository.updateStatus(metadata.id, status: .received, error: nil)
   387→                } catch {
   388→                    logError("⚠️ Failed to update metadata for assembled recording", error: error)
   389→                }
   390→            }
   391→            
   392→            recordingsReceivedCount += 1
   393→            lastReceivedRecording = savedRecording
   394→            
   395→            // Mark the chunked transfer as completed in the connectivity manager.
   396→            // Without this, the observer in setupIncomingTransferObserver() would overwrite
   397→            // our local .completed state back to .receiving (since WCM never transitions
   398→            // chunked transfers to .completed on its own).
   399→            connectivityManager.markIncomingTransferCompleted(recordingId: metadata.id)

400→            
   401→            // Update transfer info to completed.
   402→            // Set uploadProgress to 0.0 so the incoming-transfer observer's merge logic
   403→            // keeps this entry alive while the cloud upload is pending.
   404→            let completedTransfer = IncomingTransferInfo(
   405→                id: metadata.id,
   406→                metadata: metadata,
   407→                state: .completed,
   408→                uploadId: nil,
   409→                uploadProgress: 0.0
   410→            )
   411→            incomingTransfers[metadata.id] = completedTransfer
   412→            
   413→            // Mark WatchTransfer as completed in Core Data
   414→            if let repo = watchTransferRepository {
   415→                try? repo.markCompleted(metadata.id)
   416→            }
   417→            
   418→            // Send confirmation to watch (for the ENTIRE recording, not per-chunk)
   419→            // The Watch will only delete its local chunks after this full-recording confirmation
   420→            pendingConfirmations.append(metadata.id)
   421→            sendConfirmationToWatch()
   422→            
   423→            // Upload and create meeting
   424→            uploadAndCreateMeeting(recording: savedRecording)
   425→            
   426→            log("✅ ASSEMBLED RECORDING PROCESSED SUCCESSFULLY")
   427→            
   428→            // Clean up chunk assembly data (chunks files on disk)
   429→            ChunkAssemblyManager.shared.cleanupAssembly(for: metadata.id)
   430→            
   431→            // Auto-remove the transfer card from UI after a brief display period.
   432→            // The transfer itself is complete (confirmed to Watch). The ongoing upload
   433→            // status is tracked via RecordingMetadata in the recordings list.
   434→            let assembledId = metadata.id
   435→            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 3.0) { [weak self] in
   436→                guard let self = self else { return }
   437→                if let transfer = self.incomingTransfers[assembledId],
   438→                   case .completed = transfer.state {
   439→                    self.incomingTransfers.removeValue(forKey: assembledId)
   440→                    self.connectivityManager.removeIncomingTransfer(recordingId: assembledId)
   441→                    self.log("🧹 Auto-removed assembled transfer from UI: \(assembledId)")
   442→                }
   443→            }
   444→            
   445→        } catch {
   446→            logError("❌ Failed to save assembled recording", error: error)
   447→        }
   448→    }

   459→    // MARK: - Logging Helper
   460→    
   461→    /// Logs a message with timestamp and prefix
   462→    private func log(_ message: String, level: LogLevel = .info) {
   463→        let fullMessage = "\(logPrefix) \(message)"
   464→        switch level {
   465→        case .debug:
   466→            logger.debug(fullMessage)
   467→        case .info:
   468→            logger.info(fullMessage)
   469→        case .warning:
   470→            logger.warning(fullMessage)
   471→        case .error:
   472→            logger.error(fullMessage, error: nil)
   473→        case .fatal:
   474→            logger.fatal(fullMessage, error: nil)
   475→        }
   476→    }
   477→    
   478→    /// Logs an error with context
   479→    private func logError(_ message: String, error: Error?) {
   480→        logger.error("\(logPrefix) \(message)", error: error)
   481→    }

   483→    // MARK: - Pending Files Handling
   484→    
   485→    /// Sets up observer for WCSession activation state to check for pending files
   486→    private func setupActivationStateObserver() {
   487→        log("Setting up activation state observer...")
   488→        
   489→        // Listen for activation state changes to check for pending files
   490→        connectivityManager.$activationState
   491→            .receive(on: DispatchQueue.main)
   492→            .sink { [weak self] state in
   493→                guard let self = self else { return }
   494→                
   495→                self.log("WCSession activation state changed: \(state.rawValue)")
   496→                
   497→                if state == .activated && !self.hasCheckedPendingFiles {
   498→                    self.hasCheckedPendingFiles = true
   499→                    self.log("Session activated - checking for pending files in 1 second...")
   500→                    // Give a brief delay for any pending delegate calls to come through
   501→                    DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 1.0) {
   502→                        self.checkForPendingReceivedFiles()
   503→                    }
   504→                }
   505→            }
   506→            .store(in: &cancellables)
   507→    }
   508→    
   509→    /// Checks for any files that were received while the app was terminated.
   510→    /// WCSession delivers pending files through the delegate after activation,
   511→    /// but we also check the hasContentPending flag for awareness.
   512→    /// Call this on app launch after WCSession is activated.
   513→    func checkForPendingReceivedFiles() {
   514→        log("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════")
   515→        log("CHECKING FOR PENDING RECEIVED FILES")
   516→        log("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════")
   517→        
   518→        guard WCSession.isSupported() else {
   519→            log("⚠️ WCSession not supported on this device")
   520→            return
   521→        }
   522→        
   523→        let session = WCSession.default
   524→        
   525→        guard session.activationState == .activated else {
   526→            log("⏳ WCSession not activated yet - will check later")
   527→            return
   528→        }
   529→        
   530→        // Check if there's pending content (files received while app was terminated)
   531→        // Note: hasContentPending is true if there are transfers/messages waiting
   532→        #if os(iOS)
   533→        let hasContentPending = session.hasContentPending
   534→        log("Has content pending: \(hasContentPending)")
   535→        
   536→        self.hasPendingFiles = hasContentPending
   537→        
   538→        if hasContentPending {
   539→            log("📥 Pending content detected - delegate methods will deliver files")
   540→            log("Waiting for WCSession delegate callbacks...")
   541→        } else {
   542→            log("✓ No pending files to process")
   543→        }
   544→        #endif
   545→        
   546→        log("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════")
   547→    }
   548→    
   549→    /// Forces a re-check for pending files. Call this if you suspect files were missed.
   550→    func forceCheckPendingFiles() {
   551→        log("Force re-checking for pending files...")
   552→        hasCheckedPendingFiles = false
   553→        checkForPendingReceivedFiles()
   554→        hasCheckedPendingFiles = true
   555→    }

   565→            self.repository = repository

   573→    
   574→    /// Sets up the callback on WatchConnectivityManager for receiving recording files
   575→    private func setupRecordingReceptionCallback() {
   576→        logger.debug("🔵 [WatchRecordingService] Setting up recording reception callback...")
   577→        log("Setting up recording reception callback...")
   578→        
   579→        // Set up the callback for when files are received
   580→        connectivityManager.onRecordingReceived = { [weak self] result in
   581→            Task { @MainActor in
   582→                switch result {
   583→                case .success((let fileURL, let metadata)):
   584→                    self?.logger.info("[WatchRecordingService] onRecordingReceived — file: \(fileURL.lastPathComponent), recordingId: \(metadata.id), isChunked: \(metadata.isChunked)")
   585→                    self?.handleReceivedRecording(fileURL: fileURL, metadata: metadata)
   586→                case .failure(let error):
   587→                    self?.logger.error("[WatchRecordingService] onRecordingReceived failed: \(error.localizedDescription)", error: error)
   588→                    self?.isProcessingTransfer = false
   589→                }
   590→            }
   591→        }
   592→        
   593→        logger.info("[WatchRecordingService] Recording reception callback registered")
   594→        
   595→        // Also listen for the notification-based approach
   596→        NotificationCenter.default.publisher(for: .recordingDidReceiveFromWatch)
   597→            .receive(on: DispatchQueue.main)
   598→            .sink { [weak self] notification in
   599→                guard let fileURL = notification.userInfo?["fileURL"] as? URL,

600→                
   601→                // Handle request to open iPhone app
   602→                if action == WatchMessageKey.actionOpenIPhoneApp {
   603→                    self.logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] iPhone requests user to open iPhone app")
   604→                    NotificationCenter.default.post(name: .iPhoneRequestsOpenApp, object: nil, userInfo: message)
   605→                }
   606→                
   607→                if action == WatchMessageKey.actionRetryTransferRequest {
   608→                    self.logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] iPhone requested transfer retry")
   609→                    NotificationCenter.default.post(name: .iPhoneRequestedTransferRetry, object: nil, userInfo: message)
   610→                }
   611→            }
   612→            #endif
   613→            
   614→            #if os(iOS)
   615→            // Handle Watch transfer coordination messages
   616→            if let action = message[WatchMessageKey.action] as? String {
   617→                switch action {
   618→                case WatchMessageKey.actionPrepareForTransfer:
   619→                    // Watch is about to send chunks -- acknowledge readiness
   620→                    self.logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Watch preparing to transfer, sending acknowledgment")
   621→                    NotificationCenter.default.post(name: .watchPreparingTransfer, object: nil, userInfo: message)
   622→                    
   623→                case WatchMessageKey.actionCleanupPartials:
   624→                    // Watch hit complete-fail -- clean up partial data
   625→                    if let recordingIdString = message[WatchMessageKey.recordingId] as? String {
   626→                        self.logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Watch requested cleanup of partials for: \(recordingIdString)")
   627→                        NotificationCenter.default.post(name: .watchRequestedCleanup, object: nil, userInfo: message)
   628→                    }
   629→                    
   630→                default:
   631→                    self.logger.debug("[WatchConnectivity] Unknown action from Watch: \(action)")
   632→                }
   633→            }
   634→            #endif
   635→        }
   636→    }

   638→    func session(_ session: WCSession, didReceiveApplicationContext applicationContext: [String : Any]) {
   639→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
   640→            // Update cached context on main thread (thread-safe access)
   641→            self.cachedApplicationContext = applicationContext
   642→            self.lastContext = applicationContext
   643→            
   644→            #if os(watchOS)
   645→            // Handle authentication token sync on watchOS
   646→            self.handleReceivedAuthContext(applicationContext)
   647→            
   648→            // Handle recording transfer confirmations (legacy -- kept for backward compatibility)
   649→            self.handleTransferConfirmation(applicationContext)
   650→            
   651→            // Handle iOS processing status updates
   652→            self.handleiOSProcessingStatusUpdates(applicationContext)
   653→            #endif
   654→            
   655→            #if os(iOS)
   656→            // Handle transfer progress updates from watch
   657→            self.handleReceivedProgressUpdates(applicationContext)
   658→            // Handle recording-on-Watch indicator (for Live Activity and Profile banner)
   659→            self.handleRecordingOnWatchContext(applicationContext)
   660→            // Handle microphone permission status from watch
   661→            self.handleWatchMicrophonePermissionContext(applicationContext)
   662→            #endif
   663→        }
   664→    }
   665→    
   666→    /// Called when user info is received via transferUserInfo (queued delivery).
   667→    /// Confirmations are now sent via transferUserInfo instead of applicationContext
   668→    /// to ensure guaranteed delivery without being overwritten by other context updates.
   669→    func session(_ session: WCSession, didReceiveUserInfo userInfo: [String: Any] = [:]) {
   670→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
   671→            #if os(watchOS)
   672→            // Handle recording transfer confirmations delivered via transferUserInfo.
   673→            // This is the preferred path -- applicationContext confirmations are kept
   674→            // only for backward compatibility with older iOS app versions.
   675→            self.handleTransferConfirmation(userInfo)
   676→            #endif
   677→            
   678→            #if os(iOS)
   679→            // Currently no iOS-side handling needed for transferUserInfo from Watch.
   680→            // Watch uses applicationContext for progress updates (last-value-wins is fine for progress).
   681→            self.logger.debug("[WatchConnectivity] Received transferUserInfo on iOS: \(userInfo.keys)")
   682→            #endif
   683→        }
   684→    }
   685→
   686→    #if os(iOS)
   687→    func sessionDidBecomeInactive(_ session: WCSession) {
   688→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
   689→            self.updateConnectionStatus()
   690→        }
   691→    }
   692→
   693→    func sessionDidDeactivate(_ session: WCSession) {
   694→        session.activate()
   695→    }
   696→    
   697→    // NEW: Track watch state changes
   698→    func sessionWatchStateDidChange(_ session: WCSession) {
   699→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
   700→            self.updateConnectionStatus()
   701→        }
   702→    }
   703→    #endif
   704→}
