   731→// MARK: - Context Keys for Watch Communication
   732→/// Keys used for application context communication between iOS and watchOS
   733→enum WatchContextKey {
   734→    static let accessToken = "accessToken"
   735→    static let isAuthenticated = "isAuthenticated"
   736→    
   737→    // User identity keys (synced from iOS after fetchMe)
   738→    static let userId = "userId"
   739→    static let userEmail = "userEmail"
   740→    
   741→    // Recording transfer keys
   742→    static let transferredRecordingIds = "transferredRecordingIds"
   743→    static let transferredRecordingAckPhase = "transferredRecordingAckPhase"
   744→    static let pendingDeletions = "pendingDeletions"
   745→    
   746→    // Transfer progress keys (watch -> iOS)
   747→    static let transferProgressUpdates = "transferProgressUpdates" // Dictionary: [recordingId: progress]
   748→    static let transferMetadata = "transferMetadata" // Dictionary: [recordingId: metadataDict]
   749→    
   750→    // Recording-on-Watch indicator (watch -> iOS)
   751→    static let isRecordingOnWatch = "isRecordingOnWatch"
   752→    static let recordingStartedAt = "recordingStartedAt"
   753→    
   754→    // Microphone permission status (watch -> iOS)
   755→    // Synced so the iPhone can gate the user until mic permission is granted on the Watch
   756→    static let microphonePermissionGranted = "microphonePermissionGranted"
   757→}
   758→
   759→/// Phase of iPhone -> Watch transfer confirmation.
   760→/// - `received`: iPhone durably received/saved transfer payload.
   761→/// - `processed`: iPhone finished downstream processing (upload + meeting creation).
   762→enum RecordingTransferConfirmationPhase: String {
   763→    case received
   764→    case processed
   765→}
   766→
   767→// MARK: - Recording Transfer Metadata
   768→
   769→/// Metadata sent alongside recording file transfers from watch to iOS
   770→/// This struct is Codable to allow serialization in the file transfer metadata dictionary
   771→public struct RecordingTransferMetadata: Codable {
   772→    public let id: UUID
   773→    public let filename: String
   774→    public let duration: TimeInterval
   775→    public let createdAt: Date
   776→    /// When the recording actually started (for sending to backend)
   777→    public let startTime: Date
   778→    
   779→    /// Optional chunk information. When nil, this is a legacy single-file transfer.
   780→    /// When present, this file is one chunk of a larger recording that will be
   781→    /// assembled on the iPhone before upload.
   782→    public let chunkInfo: ChunkTransferMetadata?
   783→    
   784→    /// True if this transfer is part of a chunked recording
   785→    public var isChunked: Bool {
   786→        return chunkInfo != nil
   787→    }
   788→    
   789→    public init(id: UUID, filename: String, duration: TimeInterval, createdAt: Date, startTime: Date, chunkInfo: ChunkTransferMetadata? = nil) {
   790→        self.id = id
   791→        self.filename = filename
   792→        self.duration = duration
   793→        self.createdAt = createdAt
   794→        self.startTime = startTime
   795→        self.chunkInfo = chunkInfo
   796→    }
   797→    
   798→    /// Converts the metadata to a dictionary for WCSession file transfer
   799→    func toDictionary() -> [String: Any] {
   800→        var dict: [String: Any] = [
   801→            "id": id.uuidString,
   802→            "filename": filename,
   803→            "duration": duration,
   804→            "createdAt": createdAt.timeIntervalSince1970,
   805→            "startTime": startTime.timeIntervalSince1970
   806→        ]
   807→        
   808→        // Include chunk info if this is a chunked transfer
   809→        if let chunkInfo = chunkInfo {
   810→            dict["chunkInfo"] = chunkInfo.toDictionary()
   811→        }
   812→        
   813→        return dict
   814→    }
   815→    
   816→    /// Creates metadata from a dictionary received via WCSession file transfer
   817→    static func fromDictionary(_ dict: [String: Any]) -> RecordingTransferMetadata? {
   818→        guard let idString = dict["id"] as? String,
   819→              let id = UUID(uuidString: idString),
   820→              let filename = dict["filename"] as? String,
   821→              let duration = dict["duration"] as? TimeInterval,
   822→              let createdAtTimestamp = dict["createdAt"] as? TimeInterval else {
   823→            return nil
   824→        }
   825→        
   826→        // Use startTime if available, otherwise fallback to createdAt for backwards compatibility
   827→        let startTimeTimestamp = dict["startTime"] as? TimeInterval ?? createdAtTimestamp
   828→        
   829→        return RecordingTransferMetadata(
   830→            id: id,
   831→            filename: filename,
   832→            duration: duration,
   833→            createdAt: Date(timeIntervalSince1970: createdAtTimestamp),
   834→            startTime: Date(timeIntervalSince1970: startTimeTimestamp),
   835→            chunkInfo: ChunkTransferMetadata.fromDictionary(dict["chunkInfo"] as? [String: Any])
   836→        )
   837→    }
   838→}
   839→
   840→// MARK: - Chunk Transfer Metadata
   841→
   842→/// Metadata for a single chunk of a longer recording that has been split
   843→/// for reliable transfer. When `chunkInfo` is nil on `RecordingTransferMetadata`,
   844→/// the transfer is a legacy single-file recording (backward compatible).
   845→public struct ChunkTransferMetadata: Codable {
   846→    /// The logical recording ID that all chunks belong to
   847→    public let parentRecordingId: UUID
   848→    
   849→    /// Unique ID for this specific chunk
   850→    public let chunkId: UUID
   851→    
   852→    /// 0-based index indicating the order of this chunk in the recording
   853→    public let chunkIndex: Int
   854→    
   855→    /// True if this is the final chunk (recording has stopped)
   856→    public let isLastChunk: Bool
   857→    
   858→    /// Duration of audio in this specific chunk (seconds)
   859→    public let chunkDuration: TimeInterval
   860→    
   861→    /// Total accumulated recording duration including this chunk (seconds)
   862→    public let totalDurationSoFar: TimeInterval
   863→    
   864→    /// When the overall recording started (not when this chunk started)
   865→    public let recordingStartTime: Date
   866→    
   867→    public init(
   868→        parentRecordingId: UUID,
   869→        chunkId: UUID,
   870→        chunkIndex: Int,
   871→        isLastChunk: Bool,
   872→        chunkDuration: TimeInterval,
   873→        totalDurationSoFar: TimeInterval,
   874→        recordingStartTime: Date
   875→    ) {
   876→        self.parentRecordingId = parentRecordingId
   877→        self.chunkId = chunkId
   878→        self.chunkIndex = chunkIndex
   879→        self.isLastChunk = isLastChunk
   880→        self.chunkDuration = chunkDuration
   881→        self.totalDurationSoFar = totalDurationSoFar
   882→        self.recordingStartTime = recordingStartTime
   883→    }
   884→    
   885→    /// Converts to dictionary for WCSession file transfer metadata
   886→    func toDictionary() -> [String: Any] {
   887→        return [
   888→            "parentRecordingId": parentRecordingId.uuidString,
   889→            "chunkId": chunkId.uuidString,
   890→            "chunkIndex": chunkIndex,
   891→            "isLastChunk": isLastChunk,
   892→            "chunkDuration": chunkDuration,
   893→            "totalDurationSoFar": totalDurationSoFar,
   894→            "recordingStartTime": recordingStartTime.timeIntervalSince1970
   895→        ]
   896→    }
   897→    
   898→    /// Creates from dictionary received via WCSession file transfer
   899→    static func fromDictionary(_ dict: [String: Any]?) -> ChunkTransferMetadata? {

800→                guard let self = self else { return }
   801→                self.handleTransferPreparation(notification: notification)
   802→            }
   803→            .store(in: &cancellables)
   804→        
   805→        // Watch hit complete-fail — clean up partial chunk data
   806→        NotificationCenter.default.publisher(for: .watchRequestedCleanup)
   807→            .receive(on: DispatchQueue.main)
   808→            .sink { [weak self] notification in
   809→                guard let self = self else { return }
   810→                self.handleCleanupRequest(notification: notification)
   811→            }
   812→            .store(in: &cancellables)
   813→        
   814→        log("Transfer coordination observers configured")
   815→    }
   816→    
   817→    /// Handles the Watch's "prepare for transfer" message.
   818→    /// Sends an acknowledgment back so the Watch knows it can start sending chunks.
   819→    /// - Parameter notification: Contains recording metadata (recordingId, totalChunks, etc.)
   820→    private func handleTransferPreparation(notification: Notification) {
   821→        guard let userInfo = notification.userInfo,
   822→              let recordingIdString = userInfo[WatchMessageKey.recordingId] as? String else {
   823→            log("Received prepareForTransfer without recordingId")
   824→            return
   825→        }
   826→        
   827→        log("Received transfer preparation request for recording: \(recordingIdString)")
   828→        
   829→        // Send acknowledgment to Watch that we're ready to receive
   830→        let ackMessage: [String: Any] = [
   831→            WatchMessageKey.action: WatchMessageKey.actionTransferAcknowledged,
   832→            WatchMessageKey.recordingId: recordingIdString
   833→        ]
   834→        
   835→        connectivityManager.sendMessage(ackMessage) { [weak self] error in
   836→            if let error = error {
   837→                self?.logError("Failed to send transfer acknowledgment", error: error)
   838→            } else {
   839→                self?.log("Transfer acknowledgment sent to Watch")
   840→            }
   841→        }
   842→    }
   843→    
   844→    /// Handles the Watch's "cleanup partials" request.
   845→    /// Deletes all partial chunk data and metadata for a recording that hit complete-fail.
   846→    /// - Parameter notification: Contains the recordingId to clean up
   847→    private func handleCleanupRequest(notification: Notification) {
   848→        guard let userInfo = notification.userInfo,
   849→              let recordingIdString = userInfo[WatchMessageKey.recordingId] as? String,
   850→              let recordingId = UUID(uuidString: recordingIdString) else {
   851→            log("Received cleanup request without valid recordingId")
   852→            return
   853→        }
   854→        
   855→        log("Cleaning up partial data for recording: \(recordingId)")
   856→        
   857→        // Clean up chunk assembly data
   858→        ChunkAssemblyManager.shared.cleanupAssembly(for: recordingId)
   859→        
   860→        // Clean up metadata if it exists
   861→        if let metadataRepo = metadataRepository {
   862→            do {
   863→                if let metadata = try metadataRepo.fetchById(recordingId) {
   864→                    try metadataRepo.delete(recordingId)
   865→                    log("Deleted metadata for recording: \(recordingId)")
   866→                }
   867→            } catch {
   868→                logError("Failed to cleanup metadata for recording: \(recordingId)", error: error)
   869→            }
   870→        }
   871→        
   872→        // Remove from incoming transfers UI
   873→        incomingTransfers.removeValue(forKey: recordingId)
   874→        
   875→        log("Cleanup complete for recording: \(recordingId) — Watch will resend all chunks")
   876→    }
   877→    
   878→    // MARK: - Stall Detection (iPhone-Side)
   879→    
   880→    /// Starts a repeating timer that checks for transfers stuck without progress updates.
   881→    /// Fires every 60 seconds. Timeout budget is duration-aware via `TransferTimeoutPolicy`
   882→    /// (3 min per 30 min bucket). The Watch has its own parallel stall detection;
   883→    /// this acts as a safety net on the iPhone side.
   884→    private func setupStallDetectionTimer() {
   885→        stallCheckTimer?.invalidate()
   886→        stallCheckTimer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: 60, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
   887→            Task { @MainActor in
   888→                self?.checkForStalledIncomingTransfers()
   889→            }
   890→        }
   891→        log("✅ Stall detection timer started (60s interval, duration-aware timeout)")
   892→    }
   893→    
   894→    /// Iterates all incoming transfers and fails any that have been stuck for too long.
   895→    /// Also checks Core Data for:
   896→    /// - Orphaned transfers (progress 100% in DB but file never arrived — no RecordingMetadata)
   897→    /// - Chunk assemblies stuck in `.receiving` for > 1 hour.
   898→    private func checkForStalledIncomingTransfers() {
   899→        let now = Date()
   900→        
   901→        for (recordingId, transfer) in incomingTransfers {
   902→            // Only check active transfers (pending / receiving); skip completed or already-failed
   903→            switch transfer.state {
   904→            case .pending, .receiving:
   905→                break
   906→            case .completed, .failed:
   907→                continue
   908→            }
   909→            
   910→            guard let lastUpdate = transfer.lastProgressUpdateTime else { continue }
   911→            
   912→            let elapsed = now.timeIntervalSince(lastUpdate)
   913→            let timeout = transferTimeoutPolicy.timeout(forRecordingDuration: transfer.totalRecordingDuration)
   914→            guard elapsed >= timeout else { continue }
   915→            
   916→            let elapsedMinutes = Int(elapsed / 60)
   917→            log("⏰ Transfer \(recordingId) stalled — no progress for \(elapsedMinutes) min. Marking as failed.")
   918→            handleStalledIncomingTransfer(recordingId: recordingId)
   919→        }
   920→        
   921→        // Orphaned transfer check: DB shows receiving with old updatedAt but no RecordingMetadata.
   922→        // File was never saved (e.g. progress reached 100% via context but didReceive file never fired).
   923→        if let repo = watchTransferRepository, let metadataRepo = metadataRepository {
   924→            if let active = try? repo.fetchActive() {
   925→                for transfer in active {
   926→                    guard let recordingId = transfer.id else { continue }
   927→                    guard transfer.statusEnum == .receiving else { continue }
   928→                    let timeout = transferTimeoutPolicy.timeout(forRecordingDuration: transfer.duration)
   929→                    let staleCutoff = now.addingTimeInterval(-timeout)
   930→                    guard (transfer.updatedAt ?? transfer.createdAt ?? now) < staleCutoff else { continue }
   931→                    // No RecordingMetadata means file was never received and saved.
   932→                    guard (try? metadataRepo.fetchById(recordingId)) == nil else { continue }
   933→                    let orphanError = "Transfer stalled at 100% — file never arrived. Tap Retry to resend."
   934→                    log("⏰ Orphaned transfer detected: \(recordingId) — \(orphanError)")
   935→                    handleStalledIncomingTransfer(recordingId: recordingId, customError: orphanError)
   936→                }
   937→            }
   938→        }
   939→        
   940→        // Stale assembly timeout: chunk assemblies stuck in `.receiving` for > 1 hour.
   941→        if let repo = watchTransferRepository {
   942→            let oneHourAgo = now.addingTimeInterval(-3600)
   943→            if let staleAssemblies = try? repo.fetchStaleAssemblies(olderThan: oneHourAgo) {
   944→                for transfer in staleAssemblies {
   945→                    guard let recordingId = transfer.id else { continue }
   946→                    log("⏰ Stale chunk assembly detected: \(recordingId) — no chunk for > 1 hour. Timing out.")
   947→                    try? repo.markTimedOut(recordingId)
   948→                    handleStalledIncomingTransfer(recordingId: recordingId)
   949→                }
   950→            }
   951→        }
   952→    }
   953→    
   954→    /// Handles a single stalled incoming transfer:
   955→    /// 1. Marks it as failed in both WatchRecordingService and WatchConnectivityManager
   956→    /// 2. Cleans up partial chunk assembly data
   957→    /// 3. Ends the associated Live Activity
   958→    /// 4. Keeps failed row visible for manual retry/dismiss
   959→    /// - Parameters:
   960→    ///   - recordingId: The UUID of the stalled recording
   961→    ///   - customError: Optional custom error message (e.g. for orphaned "100% but file never arrived" case)
   962→    private func handleStalledIncomingTransfer(recordingId: UUID, customError: String? = nil) {
   963→        let timeoutSeconds = transferTimeoutSeconds(for: recordingId)
   964→        let timeoutMinutes = Int(timeoutSeconds / 60)
   965→        let errorMessage = customError ?? "Transfer timed out — no progress for \(timeoutMinutes) minutes. Retry will start from scratch."
   966→        
   967→        // 0. Persist failure in Core Data
   968→        if let repo = watchTransferRepository {
   969→            try? repo.markFailed(recordingId, error: errorMessage)
   970→        }
   971→        
   972→        // 1. Mark as failed in WatchConnectivityManager so the observer doesn't overwrite us
   973→        connectivityManager.markIncomingTransferTimedOut(
   974→            recordingId: recordingId,
   975→            error: errorMessage
   976→        )
   977→        
   978→        // 2. Update our local transfer info
   979→        let existing = incomingTransfers[recordingId]
   980→        let failedTransfer = IncomingTransferInfo(
   981→            id: recordingId,
   982→            metadata: existing?.metadata,
   983→            state: .failed(errorMessage),
   984→            uploadId: existing?.uploadId,
   985→            uploadProgress: existing?.uploadProgress
   986→        )
   987→        incomingTransfers[recordingId] = failedTransfer
   988→        
   989→        // 3. Timeouts must retry from scratch, so remove all partial assembly state.
   990→        ChunkAssemblyManager.shared.cleanupAssembly(for: recordingId)
   991→        
   992→        // 4. End Live Activity with failure
   993→        liveActivityManager.endActivity(
   994→            recordingId: recordingId,
   995→            success: false,
   996→            errorMessage: errorMessage
   997→        )
   998→
   999→        Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
  1000→            await self?.transferFailureNotificationService.notifyTransferFailed(recordingId: recordingId)
  1001→        }
  1002→        
  1003→        logError("❌ Transfer timed out for recording: \(recordingId)", error: nil)
  1004→        
  1005→        // 5. Keep failed row visible so user can inspect details and retry.
  1006→    }
  1007→
  1008→    private func transferTimeoutSeconds(for recordingId: UUID) -> TimeInterval {
  1009→        if let transfer = incomingTransfers[recordingId] {
  1010→            return transferTimeoutPolicy.timeout(forRecordingDuration: transfer.totalRecordingDuration)
  1011→        }
  1012→        if let repo = watchTransferRepository,
  1013→           let transfer = try? repo.fetchById(recordingId) {
  1014→            return transferTimeoutPolicy.timeout(forRecordingDuration: transfer.duration)
  1015→        }
  1016→        return transferTimeoutPolicy.timeout(forRecordingDuration: nil)
  1017→    }
  1018→
  1019→    /// Prepares local iPhone state so manual retry from Watch starts from scratch.
  1020→    /// Clears partial chunk assembly and resets incoming transfer progress guards.
  1021→    func prepareManualRetryFromScratch(recordingId: UUID) {
  1022→        ChunkAssemblyManager.shared.cleanupAssembly(for: recordingId)
  1023→        connectivityManager.prepareIncomingTransferRetry(recordingId: recordingId)
  1024→        log("Prepared manual retry from scratch for recording: \(recordingId)")
  1025→    }

  1027→    // MARK: - Recording Reception
  1028→    
  1029→    /// Handles a recording file received from the Apple Watch.
  1030→    ///
  1031→    /// If the transfer contains chunk info, the file is routed to `ChunkAssemblyManager`
  1032→    /// for progressive assembly. Otherwise, it follows the existing single-file flow.
  1033→    ///
  1034→    /// Flow (single-file): Receive → Save → Upload → Confirm
  1035→    /// Flow (chunked):     Receive → AssemblyManager → (all chunks) → Merge → Upload → Confirm
  1036→    ///
  1037→    /// - Parameters:
  1038→    ///   - fileURL: The temporary URL where the transferred file is located
  1039→    ///   - metadata: The recording metadata from the watch
  1040→    private func handleReceivedRecording(fileURL: URL, metadata: RecordingTransferMetadata) {
  1041→        logger.info("[WatchRecordingService] Receiving recording from Watch — id: \(metadata.id), filename: \(metadata.filename), duration: \(String(format: "%.2f", metadata.duration))s, isChunked: \(metadata.isChunked)")
  1042→        
  1043→        // ========================================
  1044→        // DUPLICATE DELIVERY GUARD
  1045→        // WatchConnectivityManager fires both `onRecordingReceived` callback AND
  1046→        // `.recordingDidReceiveFromWatch` notification for the same incoming file.
  1047→        // Primary: Core Data-based dedup (survives app restarts).
  1048→        // Fallback: in-memory Set for the window before configure() is called.
  1049→        // ========================================

900→        guard let dict = dict,
   901→              let parentIdString = dict["parentRecordingId"] as? String,
   902→              let parentId = UUID(uuidString: parentIdString),
   903→              let chunkIdString = dict["chunkId"] as? String,
   904→              let chunkId = UUID(uuidString: chunkIdString),
   905→              let chunkIndex = dict["chunkIndex"] as? Int,
   906→              let isLastChunk = dict["isLastChunk"] as? Bool,
   907→              let chunkDuration = dict["chunkDuration"] as? TimeInterval,
   908→              let totalDurationSoFar = dict["totalDurationSoFar"] as? TimeInterval,
   909→              let startTimestamp = dict["recordingStartTime"] as? TimeInterval else {
   910→            return nil
   911→        }
   912→        
   913→        return ChunkTransferMetadata(
   914→            parentRecordingId: parentId,
   915→            chunkId: chunkId,
   916→            chunkIndex: chunkIndex,
   917→            isLastChunk: isLastChunk,
   918→            chunkDuration: chunkDuration,
   919→            totalDurationSoFar: totalDurationSoFar,
   920→            recordingStartTime: Date(timeIntervalSince1970: startTimestamp)
   921→        )
   922→    }
   923→}

   948→// MARK: - Authentication Token Sync
   949→
   950→extension WatchConnectivityManager {
   951→    
   952→    #if os(iOS)
   953→    /// Syncs the access token and user identity to the paired Apple Watch via application context.
   954→    /// The context will be delivered to the watch even if it's not immediately reachable.
   955→    /// IMPORTANT: Preserves recording confirmations when updating auth context.
   956→    /// - Parameters:
   957→    ///   - token: The access token to send to the watch. Pass nil to clear the token on the watch.
   958→    ///   - userId: The authenticated user's ID (from fetchMe). Included so watch can tag Sentry events.
   959→    ///   - userEmail: The authenticated user's email (from fetchMe). Included so watch can tag Sentry events.
   960→    ///   - completion: Optional completion handler called on main queue with success/failure result.
   961→    func syncAccessToken(
   962→        _ token: String?,
   963→        userId: String? = nil,
   964→        userEmail: String? = nil,
   965→        completion: ((Result<Void, Error>) -> Void)? = nil
   966→    ) {
   967→        if Thread.isMainThread {
   968→            performSyncAccessToken(token: token, userId: userId, userEmail: userEmail, completion: completion)
   969→        } else {
   970→            DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   971→                self?.performSyncAccessToken(token: token, userId: userId, userEmail: userEmail, completion: completion)
   972→            }
   973→        }
   974→    }
   975→    
   976→    /// Internal method to perform access token sync (must be called on main thread)
   977→    private func performSyncAccessToken(
   978→        token: String?,
   979→        userId: String?,
   980→        userEmail: String?,
   981→        completion: ((Result<Void, Error>) -> Void)?
   982→    ) {
   983→        dispatchPrecondition(condition: .onQueue(.main))
   984→        
   985→        // Get authoritative context from cached copy
   986→        var context = getCachedApplicationContextSafely()
   987→        
   988→        // Update auth-related keys
   989→        context[WatchContextKey.isAuthenticated] = token != nil
   990→        
   991→        if let token = token {
   992→            context[WatchContextKey.accessToken] = token
   993→        } else {
   994→            context.removeValue(forKey: WatchContextKey.accessToken)
   995→        }
   996→        
   997→        // Sync user identity for Sentry tagging on the watch
   998→        if let userId = userId {
   999→            context[WatchContextKey.userId] = userId
  1000→        } else {
  1001→            context.removeValue(forKey: WatchContextKey.userId)
  1002→        }
  1003→        if let userEmail = userEmail {
  1004→            context[WatchContextKey.userEmail] = userEmail
  1005→        } else {
  1006→            context.removeValue(forKey: WatchContextKey.userEmail)
  1007→        }
  1008→        
  1009→        // Preserve transferredRecordingIds from existing context
  1010→        // This prevents overwriting recording confirmations when auth state changes
  1011→        
  1012→        // Use application context for reliable delivery even when watch is not reachable
  1013→        updateApplicationContext(context) { [weak self] error in
  1014→            guard let self = self else { return }
  1015→            if let error = error {
  1016→                self.logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] Failed to sync access token: \(error.localizedDescription)", error: error)
  1017→                completion?(.failure(error))
  1018→            } else {
  1019→                self.logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Access token synced to watch (confirmations preserved)")
  1020→                completion?(.success(()))
  1021→            }
  1022→        }
  1023→    }
  1024→    
  1025→    /// Clears the authentication token on the paired Apple Watch.
  1026→    /// Call this when the user signs out.
  1027→    func clearWatchAuthToken(completion: ((Result<Void, Error>) -> Void)? = nil) {
  1028→        syncAccessToken(nil, completion: completion)
  1029→    }
  1030→    #endif
  1031→    
  1032→    #if os(watchOS)
  1033→    /// Handles received authentication context from the iOS app.
  1034→    /// Stores or clears the access token in the watch's Keychain.
  1035→    /// - Parameter context: The application context dictionary received from iOS
  1036→    func handleReceivedAuthContext(_ context: [String: Any]) {
  1037→        let isAuthenticated = context[WatchContextKey.isAuthenticated] as? Bool ?? false
  1038→        
  1039→        if isAuthenticated, let token = context[WatchContextKey.accessToken] as? String {
  1040→            // Store the token securely in Keychain
  1041→            let success = WatchKeychainHelper.shared.setAccessToken(token)
  1042→            if success {
  1043→                logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Access token received and stored successfully")
  1044→            } else {
  1045→                logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] Failed to store received access token", error: nil)
  1046→            }
  1047→            
  1048→            // Tag Sentry with user identity synced from iOS (from fetchMe response)
  1049→            let userId = context[WatchContextKey.userId] as? String
  1050→            let userEmail = context[WatchContextKey.userEmail] as? String
  1051→            setSentryUser(id: userId, email: userEmail)
  1052→        } else {
  1053→            // Clear the stored token (user signed out or token cleared)
  1054→            WatchKeychainHelper.shared.clearAccessToken()
  1055→            logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Access token cleared on watch")
  1056→            
  1057→            // Clear Sentry user on sign-out
  1058→            logger.setUser(id: nil, email: nil)
  1059→        }
  1060→        
  1061→        // Post notification for any observers that need to react to auth state changes
  1062→        NotificationCenter.default.post(
  1063→            name: .watchAuthStateDidChange,
  1064→            object: nil,
  1065→            userInfo: [WatchContextKey.isAuthenticated: isAuthenticated]
  1066→        )
  1067→    }
  1068→    
  1069→    /// Sets the Sentry user identity on the watch side.
  1070→    /// Called when auth context is received from iOS and on app launch from cached context.
  1071→    func setSentryUser(id: String?, email: String?) {
  1072→        guard id != nil else {
  1073→            logger.warning("[WatchConnectivity] No userId available for Sentry tagging")
  1074→            return
  1075→        }
  1076→        
  1077→        logger.setUser(id: id, email: email)
  1078→        logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Sentry user set: id=\(id ?? "nil")")
  1079→    }
  1080→    
  1081→    /// Retrieves the currently stored access token on the watch
  1082→    /// - Returns: The stored access token, or nil if not available
  1083→    func getStoredAccessToken() -> String? {
  1084→        return WatchKeychainHelper.shared.getAccessToken()
  1085→    }
  1086→    
  1087→    /// Checks if the watch has a valid access token

  1091→    
  1092→    /// Handles iOS processing status updates
  1093→    /// Called when iOS sends recording processing status via application context
  1094→    func handleiOSProcessingStatusUpdates(_ context: [String: Any]) {
  1095→        guard let statuses = context["recordingProcessingStatuses"] as? [String: [String: Any]] else {
  1096→            return
  1097→        }
  1098→        
  1099→        for (idString, statusPayload) in statuses {
  1100→            guard let recordingId = UUID(uuidString: idString),
  1101→                  let statusName = statusPayload["status"] as? String else {
  1102→                continue
  1103→            }
  1104→            
  1105→            let meetingId = statusPayload["meetingId"] as? String
  1106→            let uploadProgress = statusPayload["uploadProgress"] as? Double
  1107→            
  1108→            let progressStr = uploadProgress.map { " (\(Int($0 * 100))%)" } ?? ""
  1109→            logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Received iOS status update: \(recordingId) -> \(statusName)\(progressStr)")
  1110→            
  1111→            // Update local Core Data via notification
  1112→            NotificationCenter.default.post(
  1113→                name: .iosProcessingStatusUpdated,
  1114→                object: nil,
  1115→                userInfo: [
  1116→                    "recordingId": recordingId,
  1117→                    "status": statusName,
  1118→                    "uploadProgress": uploadProgress as Any,
  1119→                    "meetingId": meetingId as Any
  1120→                ]
  1121→            )
  1122→        }
  1123→    }
  1124→    #endif
  1125→}

  1125→                    )
  1126→                } catch {
  1127→                    logger.error("[WatchRecordingService] Failed to persist chunk arrival: \(error)")
  1128→                }
  1129→            }
  1130→            
  1131→            // Compute actual chunk progress from assembly state
  1132→            let assemblyState = ChunkAssemblyManager.shared.getAssemblyState(for: parentId)
  1133→            let (chunksReceived, totalExpected): (Int, Int?) = {
  1134→                if case .receiving(let count, let total) = assemblyState {
  1135→                    return (count, total)
  1136→                }
  1137→                return (chunkInfo.chunkIndex + 1, nil)
  1138→            }()
  1139→            
  1140→            // Calculate real progress: chunksReceived / totalExpected (or estimate)
  1141→            let chunkProgress: Double? = {
  1142→                if let total = totalExpected, total > 0 {
  1143→                    return Double(chunksReceived) / Double(total)
  1144→                }
  1145→                return nil
  1146→            }()
  1147→            
  1148→            var transferInfo = IncomingTransferInfo(
  1149→                id: parentId,
  1150→                metadata: metadata,
  1151→                state: .receiving(progress: chunkProgress),
  1152→                uploadId: nil,
  1153→                uploadProgress: nil
  1154→            )
  1155→            transferInfo.chunksReceived = chunksReceived
  1156→            transferInfo.totalExpectedChunks = totalExpected
  1157→            incomingTransfers[parentId] = transferInfo
  1158→            
  1159→            // Start Live Activity if not already active
  1160→            if !liveActivityManager.hasActivity(for: parentId) {
  1161→                liveActivityManager.startActivity(for: parentId, metadata: metadata)
  1162→            }
  1163→            
  1164→            // Push chunk progress to Live Activity
  1165→            updateLiveActivityForTransfer(recordingId: parentId, transferInfo: transferInfo)
  1166→            
  1167→            // Persist progress in Core Data
  1168→            if let repo = watchTransferRepository, let p = chunkProgress {
  1169→                try? repo.updateProgress(parentId, progress: p)
  1170→            }
  1171→            
  1172→            logger.info("[WatchRecordingService] Chunk \(chunkInfo.chunkIndex) processed — progress: \(chunksReceived)/\(totalExpected.map { String($0) } ?? "?"), totalDurationSoFar: \(chunkInfo.totalDurationSoFar)s")
  1173→            
  1174→            return
  1175→        }
  1176→        // ========================================
  1177→        // CHUNK ROUTING -- END (single-file flow below)
  1178→        // ========================================
  1179→        
  1180→        log("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════")
  1181→        log("📥 RECEIVING RECORDING FROM WATCH")
  1182→        log("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════")
  1183→        log("Recording ID: \(metadata.id)")
  1184→        log("Filename: \(metadata.filename)")
  1185→        log("Duration: \(String(format: "%.2f", metadata.duration))s")
  1186→        log("Created: \(metadata.createdAt)")
  1187→        log("Temp File: \(fileURL.path)")
  1188→        
  1189→        isProcessingTransfer = true
  1190→        
  1191→        // Persist single-file transfer in Core Data
  1192→        if let repo = watchTransferRepository {
  1193→            do {
  1194→                let _ = try repo.createOrUpdate(
  1195→                    recordingId: metadata.id,
  1196→                    isChunked: false,
  1197→                    filename: metadata.filename,
  1198→                    duration: metadata.duration,
  1199→                    startTime: metadata.startTime,
  1200→                    status: .receiving
  1201→                )
  1202→                try repo.updateProgress(metadata.id, progress: 1.0)
  1203→            } catch {
  1204→                logger.error("[WatchRecordingService] Failed to persist single-file transfer: \(error)")
  1205→            }
  1206→        }
  1207→        
  1208→        // Verify the temporary file exists
  1209→        guard FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: fileURL.path) else {
  1210→            logError("❌ ERROR: Temporary file does not existt! Path: \(fileURL.path)", error: nil)
  1211→            isProcessingTransfer = false
  1212→            return
  1213→        }
  1214→        
  1215→        // Log file size
  1216→        if let attrs = try? FileManager.default.attributesOfItem(atPath: fileURL.path),
  1217→           let size = attrs[.size] as? Int64 {
  1218→            log("📦 File size: \(ByteCountFormatter.string(fromByteCount: size, countStyle: .file))")
  1219→        }
  1220→        
  1221→        guard let repository = repository else {
  1222→            logError("❌ ERROR: Repository not initialized!", error: nil)
  1223→            log("⚠️ Transfer dropped because repository is nil. Check setupRepository() initialization.")
  1224→            isProcessingTransfer = false
  1225→            return
  1226→        }
  1227→        
  1228→        // Save the recording to permanent storage
  1229→        do {
  1230→            log("💾 Saving recording to permanent storage...")
  1231→            
  1232→            // Create RecordingMetadata if it doesn't exist
  1233→            // Metadata is only created when the actual file is received (not on progress updates)
  1234→            if let metadataRepo = metadataRepository {
  1235→                do {
  1236→                    if try metadataRepo.fetchById(metadata.id) == nil {
  1237→                        // Create new metadata - will be updated with actual file path after save
  1238→                        _ = try metadataRepo.create(recordingId: metadata.id, filePath: "receiving")
  1239→                        log("📊 Recording metadata created for new recording")
  1240→                    } else {
  1241→                        log("📊 Recording metadata already exists (retry scenario)")
  1242→                    }
  1243→                } catch {
  1244→                    logError("⚠️ Failed to create metadata", error: error)
  1245→                }
  1246→            }
  1247→            
  1248→            // Update transfer info with metadata (preserve upload tracking if present)
  1249→            if var transferInfo = incomingTransfers[metadata.id] {
  1250→                transferInfo = IncomingTransferInfo(
  1251→                    id: metadata.id,
  1252→                    metadata: metadata,
  1253→                    state: transferInfo.state,
  1254→                    uploadId: transferInfo.uploadId,
  1255→                    uploadProgress: transferInfo.uploadProgress
  1256→                )
  1257→                incomingTransfers[metadata.id] = transferInfo
  1258→            }
  1259→            
  1260→            let savedRecording = try repository.saveRecordingFromWatch(
  1261→                sourceURL: fileURL,
  1262→                id: metadata.id,
  1263→                filename: metadata.filename,
  1264→                duration: metadata.duration,
  1265→                createdAt: metadata.createdAt,
  1266→                startTime: metadata.startTime
  1267→            )
  1268→            
  1269→            log("✅ Recording saved successfully!")
  1270→            log("📍 Saved to: \(savedRecording.fileURL.path)")
  1271→            
  1272→            // Update metadata status to received and set actual file path
  1273→            if let metadataRepo = metadataRepository {
  1274→                do {
  1275→                    try metadataRepo.updateFilePath(metadata.id, filePath: savedRecording.fileURL.path)
  1276→                    try metadataRepo.updateStatus(metadata.id, status: .received, error: nil)
  1277→                    try? repository.updateStatus(metadata.id, status: .received, error: nil)
  1278→                    log("📊 Recording metadata updated to received status with file path")
  1279→                    
  1280→                    // Sync status to watch
  1281→                    WatchStatusSync().sendStatusToWatch(recordingId: metadata.id, status: .received)
  1282→                } catch {
  1283→                    logError("⚠️ Failed to update metadata status", error: error)
  1284→                }
  1285→            }
  1286→            
  1287→            recordingsReceivedCount += 1
  1288→            lastReceivedRecording = savedRecording
  1289→            isProcessingTransfer = false
  1290→            
  1291→            // Update transfer info to completed (preserve upload tracking if present).
  1292→            // Set uploadProgress to 0.0 so the incoming-transfer observer's merge logic
  1293→            // keeps this entry alive while the cloud upload is pending.
  1294→            let existingTransfer = incomingTransfers[metadata.id]
  1295→            let completedTransfer = IncomingTransferInfo(
  1296→                id: metadata.id,
  1297→                metadata: metadata,
  1298→                state: .completed,

1→import Foundation
     2→
     3→/// Shared policy for transfer timeout decisions across Watch and iPhone.
     4→/// Rule: 3 minutes for every 30 minutes of recording, rounded up by bucket.
     5→struct TransferTimeoutPolicy {
     6→    static let `default` = TransferTimeoutPolicy()
     7→
     8→    /// Timeout budget per 30-minute recording bucket.
     9→    let timeoutPerThirtyMinuteBucket: TimeInterval
    10→
    11→    /// Recording-duration bucket size.
    12→    let durationBucket: TimeInterval
    13→
    14→    init(
    15→        timeoutPerThirtyMinuteBucket: TimeInterval = 3 * 60,
    16→        durationBucket: TimeInterval = 30 * 60
    17→    ) {
    18→        self.timeoutPerThirtyMinuteBucket = timeoutPerThirtyMinuteBucket
    19→        self.durationBucket = durationBucket
    20→    }
    21→
    22→    /// Calculates timeout for a recording duration using bucketed rounding:
    23→    /// 0...30m -> 3m, 30...60m -> 6m, 60...90m -> 9m, etc.
    24→    /// Unknown/invalid durations still get the minimum 3-minute timeout.
    25→    func timeout(forRecordingDuration duration: TimeInterval?) -> TimeInterval {
    26→        let safeDuration = max(0, duration ?? 0)
    27→        let bucketCount = max(1, Int(ceil(safeDuration / durationBucket)))
    28→        return TimeInterval(bucketCount) * timeoutPerThirtyMinuteBucket
    29→    }
    30→}
    31→
    32→enum TransferFailureNotificationContent {
    33→    static let title = AppSharedText.txtTransferFailed
    34→    static let body = AppSharedText.txtTransferFailedSubTitle
    35→}

1→import Foundation
     2→import CoreData
     3→
     4→// MARK: - Protocol
     5→
     6→/// Manages persistence for incoming watch-to-iPhone transfers.
     7→/// Replaces the scattered in-memory stores (`processedChunkKeys`,
     8→/// `ChunkAssemblyManager` JSON inventory) with a single Core Data table.
     9→protocol WatchTransferRepository {
    10→    /// Creates a new transfer or updates an existing one (upsert by recording id).
    11→    @discardableResult
    12→    func createOrUpdate(
    13→        recordingId: UUID,
    14→        isChunked: Bool,
    15→        filename: String?,
    16→        duration: TimeInterval,
    17→        startTime: Date?,
    18→        status: WatchTransferStatus
    19→    ) throws -> WatchTransfer
    20→
    21→    /// Fetches a single transfer by recording id.
    22→    func fetchById(_ id: UUID) throws -> WatchTransfer?
    23→
    24→    /// Returns all transfers that are not in a terminal state (completed / timedOut).
    25→    func fetchActive() throws -> [WatchTransfer]
    26→
    27→    /// Finds chunk assemblies stuck in `.receiving` longer than `threshold`.
    28→    func fetchStaleAssemblies(olderThan threshold: Date) throws -> [WatchTransfer]
    29→
    30→    /// Finds transfers in `.failed` state eligible for merge retry.
    31→    func fetchRetryableMerges(maxRetries: Int) throws -> [WatchTransfer]
    32→
    33→    // MARK: - Status Mutations
    34→
    35→    func updateProgress(_ id: UUID, progress: Double) throws
    36→    func markReceiving(_ id: UUID) throws
    37→    func markAssembling(_ id: UUID) throws
    38→    func markAssembled(_ id: UUID) throws
    39→    func markCompleted(_ id: UUID) throws
    40→    func markFailed(_ id: UUID, error: String) throws
    41→    func markTimedOut(_ id: UUID) throws
    42→    func incrementMergeRetryCount(_ id: UUID) throws
    43→
    44→    // MARK: - Chunk Tracking
    45→
    46→    /// Records that a chunk at `chunkIndex` has arrived for `recordingId`.
    47→    /// Also sets `totalExpectedChunks` when `isLastChunk` is true.
    48→    func addChunkIndex(
    49→        recordingId: UUID,
    50→        chunkIndex: Int,
    51→        isLastChunk: Bool,
    52→        totalExpectedChunks: Int?
    53→    ) throws
    54→
    55→    /// Whether a specific chunk was already received (persisted dedup guard).
    56→    func hasReceivedChunk(recordingId: UUID, chunkIndex: Int) throws -> Bool
    57→
    58→    // MARK: - Cleanup
    59→
    60→    /// Deletes completed transfers older than `date`.
    61→    func deleteCompleted(olderThan date: Date) throws
    62→}
    63→
    64→// MARK: - Implementation
    65→
    66→final class WatchTransferRepositoryImpl: WatchTransferRepository {
    67→
    68→    private let context: NSManagedObjectContext
    69→    private let logger = LoggerFactory.makeLogger()
    70→
    71→    init(context: NSManagedObjectContext) {
    72→        self.context = context
    73→    }
    74→
    75→    // MARK: - Upsert
    76→
    77→    @discardableResult
    78→    func createOrUpdate(
    79→        recordingId: UUID,
    80→        isChunked: Bool,
    81→        filename: String?,
    82→        duration: TimeInterval,
    83→        startTime: Date?,
    84→        status: WatchTransferStatus
    85→    ) throws -> WatchTransfer {
    86→        if let existing = try fetchById(recordingId) {
    87→            // Never regress from a terminal or advanced state (e.g. Watch retry
    88→            // after confirmation was lost should not overwrite .completed → .receiving)
    89→            let currentStatus = existing.statusEnum
    90→            if currentStatus.isTerminal ||
    91→               (currentStatus == .assembled && status.rawValue < WatchTransferStatus.assembled.rawValue) ||
    92→               (currentStatus == .assembling && status == .receiving) {
    93→                logger.info("[WatchTransferRepo] Skipping status downgrade for \(recordingId): \(currentStatus) → \(status)")
    94→                existing.touch()
    95→                try context.save()
    96→                return existing
    97→            }
    98→            
    99→            existing.isChunked = isChunked
   100→            if let f = filename { existing.filename = f }
   101→            existing.duration = duration
   102→            existing.startTime = startTime
   103→            existing.statusEnum = status
   104→            existing.touch()
   105→            try context.save()
   106→            return existing
   107→        }
   108→
   109→        let transfer = WatchTransfer(context: context)
   110→        transfer.id = recordingId
   111→        transfer.isChunked = isChunked
   112→        transfer.filename = filename
   113→        transfer.duration = duration
   114→        transfer.startTime = startTime
   115→        transfer.statusEnum = status
   116→        transfer.createdAt = Date()
   117→        transfer.updatedAt = Date()
   118→        transfer.progress = 0
   119→        transfer.chunksReceived = 0
   120→        transfer.totalExpectedChunks = 0
   121→        transfer.mergeRetryCount = 0
   122→
   123→        try context.save()
   124→        logger.info("[WatchTransferRepo] Created transfer: \(recordingId)")
   125→        return transfer
   126→    }
   127→
   128→    // MARK: - Fetch
   129→
   130→    func fetchById(_ id: UUID) throws -> WatchTransfer? {
   131→        let request: NSFetchRequest<WatchTransfer> = WatchTransfer.fetchRequest()
   132→        request.predicate = NSPredicate(format: "id == %@", id as CVarArg)
   133→        request.fetchLimit = 1
   134→        return try context.fetch(request).first
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    func fetchActive() throws -> [WatchTransfer] {
   138→        let request: NSFetchRequest<WatchTransfer> = WatchTransfer.fetchRequest()
   139→        let terminalStatuses: [Int16] = [
   140→            WatchTransferStatus.completed.rawValue,
   141→            WatchTransferStatus.timedOut.rawValue
   142→        ]
   143→        request.predicate = NSPredicate(format: "NOT (status IN %@)", terminalStatuses)
   144→        request.sortDescriptors = [NSSortDescriptor(key: "createdAt", ascending: false)]
   145→        return try context.fetch(request)
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    func fetchStaleAssemblies(olderThan threshold: Date) throws -> [WatchTransfer] {
   149→        let request: NSFetchRequest<WatchTransfer> = WatchTransfer.fetchRequest()
   150→        request.predicate = NSPredicate(
   151→            format: "status == %d AND isChunked == YES AND updatedAt < %@",
   152→            WatchTransferStatus.receiving.rawValue,
   153→            threshold as NSDate
   154→        )
   155→        return try context.fetch(request)
   156→    }
   157→
   158→    func fetchRetryableMerges(maxRetries: Int) throws -> [WatchTransfer] {
   159→        let request: NSFetchRequest<WatchTransfer> = WatchTransfer.fetchRequest()
   160→        request.predicate = NSPredicate(
   161→            format: "status == %d AND mergeRetryCount < %d",
   162→            WatchTransferStatus.failed.rawValue,
   163→            maxRetries
   164→        )
   165→        return try context.fetch(request)
   166→    }
   167→
   168→    // MARK: - Status Mutations
   169→
   170→    func updateProgress(_ id: UUID, progress: Double) throws {
   171→        guard let t = try fetchById(id) else { return }
   172→        t.progress = progress
   173→        t.touch()
   174→        try context.save()
   175→    }
   176→
   177→    func markReceiving(_ id: UUID) throws {
   178→        try setStatus(id, status: .receiving)
   179→    }
   180→
   181→    func markAssembling(_ id: UUID) throws {
   182→        try setStatus(id, status: .assembling)
   183→    }
   184→
   185→    func markAssembled(_ id: UUID) throws {
   186→        try setStatus(id, status: .assembled)
   187→    }
   188→
   189→    func markCompleted(_ id: UUID) throws {
   190→        try setStatus(id, status: .completed)
   191→    }
   192→
   193→    func markFailed(_ id: UUID, error: String) throws {
   194→        guard let t = try fetchById(id) else { return }
   195→        t.statusEnum = .failed
   196→        t.errorMessage = error
   197→        t.touch()
   198→        try context.save()
   199→    }
   200→
   201→    func markTimedOut(_ id: UUID) throws {
   202→        try setStatus(id, status: .timedOut)
   203→    }
   204→
   205→    func incrementMergeRetryCount(_ id: UUID) throws {
   206→        guard let t = try fetchById(id) else { return }
   207→        t.mergeRetryCount += 1
   208→        t.touch()
   209→        try context.save()
   210→    }
   211→
   212→    // MARK: - Chunk Tracking
   213→
   214→    func addChunkIndex(
   215→        recordingId: UUID,
   216→        chunkIndex: Int,
   217→        isLastChunk: Bool,
   218→        totalExpectedChunks: Int?
   219→    ) throws {
   220→        guard let t = try fetchById(recordingId) else {
   221→            logger.warning("[WatchTransferRepo] Cannot add chunk — transfer not found: \(recordingId)")
   222→            return
   223→        }
   224→        t.addReceivedChunkIndex(chunkIndex)
   225→        if isLastChunk, let total = totalExpectedChunks {
   226→            t.totalExpectedChunks = Int16(total)
   227→        }
   228→        try context.save()
   229→    }
   230→
   231→    func hasReceivedChunk(recordingId: UUID, chunkIndex: Int) throws -> Bool {
   232→        guard let t = try fetchById(recordingId) else { return false }
   233→        return t.hasChunkIndex(chunkIndex)
   234→    }
   235→
   236→    // MARK: - Cleanup
   237→
   238→    func deleteCompleted(olderThan date: Date) throws {
   239→        let request: NSFetchRequest<WatchTransfer> = WatchTransfer.fetchRequest()
   240→        request.predicate = NSPredicate(
   241→            format: "status == %d AND updatedAt < %@",
   242→            WatchTransferStatus.completed.rawValue,
   243→            date as NSDate
   244→        )
   245→        let results = try context.fetch(request)
   246→        for item in results {
   247→            context.delete(item)
   248→        }
   249→        try context.save()
   250→        if !results.isEmpty {
   251→            logger.info("[WatchTransferRepo] Cleaned up \(results.count) completed transfer(s)")
   252→        }
   253→    }
   254→
   255→    // MARK: - Private
   256→
   257→    private func setStatus(_ id: UUID, status: WatchTransferStatus) throws {
   258→        guard let t = try fetchById(id) else { return }
   259→        t.statusEnum = status
   260→        t.touch()
   261→        try context.save()
   262→    }
   263→}
   264→

1→//
     2→//  WatchStatusSync.swift
     3→//  Memorable
     4→//
     5→//  Helper for syncing recording status from iOS to Watch
     6→//
     7→
     8→import Foundation
     9→
    10→/// Helper for syncing recording status from iOS to Watch
    11→final class WatchStatusSync {
    12→    private let connectivity = WatchConnectivityManager.shared
    13→    private let logger = LoggerFactory.makeLogger()
    14→    
    15→    /// Sends processing status update to watch with optional upload progress
    16→    /// - Parameters:
    17→    ///   - recordingId: Recording identifier
    18→    ///   - status: Current RecordingStatus
    19→    ///   - uploadProgress: Upload progress (0.0 to 1.0) - only for .uploading status
    20→    ///   - meetingId: Meeting ID when meeting is created
    21→    /// - Note: This method must be called from the main thread as it accesses @Published properties
    22→    func sendStatusToWatch(recordingId: UUID, status: RecordingStatus, uploadProgress: Double? = nil, meetingId: UUID? = nil) {
    23→        // applicationContext requires main thread access (enforced by dispatchPrecondition)
    24→        var context = connectivity.applicationContext
    25→        
    26→        // Get existing status dictionary or create new
    27→        var processingStatuses = context["recordingProcessingStatuses"] as? [String: [String: Any]] ?? [:]
    28→        
    29→        // Create status payload
    30→        var statusPayload: [String: Any] = [
    31→            "status": status.displayName,
    32→            "statusRaw": status.rawValue,
    33→            "timestamp": Date().timeIntervalSince1970
    34→        ]
    35→        
    36→        // Include upload progress for uploading status
    37→        if let progress = uploadProgress, status == .uploading {
    38→            statusPayload["uploadProgress"] = progress
    39→        }
    40→        
    41→        if let meetingId = meetingId {
    42→            statusPayload["meetingId"] = meetingId.uuidString
    43→        }
    44→        
    45→        processingStatuses[recordingId.uuidString] = statusPayload
    46→        context["recordingProcessingStatuses"] = processingStatuses
    47→        
    48→        connectivity.updateApplicationContext(context) { [weak self] error in
    49→            if let error = error {
    50→                self?.logger.error("Failed to sync status to watch: \(error)")
    51→            } else {
    52→                let progressStr = uploadProgress.map { " (\(Int($0 * 100))%)" } ?? ""
    53→                self?.logger.info("Synced status to watch: \(recordingId) -> \(status.displayName)\(progressStr)")
    54→            }
    55→        }
    56→    }
    57→    
    58→    /// Clears status for a completed recording
    59→    /// - Note: This method must be called from the main thread as it accesses @Published properties
    60→    func clearStatusFromWatch(recordingId: UUID) {
    61→        // applicationContext requires main thread access (enforced by dispatchPrecondition)
    62→        var context = connectivity.applicationContext
    63→        
    64→        guard var processingStatuses = context["recordingProcessingStatuses"] as? [String: [String: Any]] else {
    65→            return
    66→        }
    67→        
    68→        processingStatuses.removeValue(forKey: recordingId.uuidString)
    69→        
    70→        if processingStatuses.isEmpty {
    71→            context.removeValue(forKey: "recordingProcessingStatuses")
    72→        } else {
    73→            context["recordingProcessingStatuses"] = processingStatuses
    74→        }
    75→        
    76→        connectivity.updateApplicationContext(context, completion: nil)
    77→    }
    78→}
    79→

1→import Foundation
     2→import Combine
     3→import SwiftUI
     4→import AVFoundation
     5→
     6→// MARK: - Logger for Recording
     7→
     8→/// Logger wrapper for recording operations with Sentry integration
     9→/// Provides convenience methods that match the existing RecordingLogger API
    10→private enum RecordingLogger {
    11→    /// Shared logger instance
    12→    private static let logger = LoggerFactory.makeLogger()
    13→    
    14→    /// Logs a debug/info message
    15→    /// - Parameters:
    16→    ///   - message: The message to log
    17→    ///   - function: The function name (automatically captured via #function)
    18→    static func log(_ message: String, function: String = #function) {
    19→        logger.info("[RecordingLogger] [\(function)] \(message)")
    20→    }
    21→    
    22→    /// Logs an error message
    23→    /// - Parameters:
    24→    ///   - message: The error message to log
    25→    ///   - error: Optional error object
    26→    ///   - function: The function name (automatically captured via #function)
    27→    static func error(_ message: String, error: Error? = nil, function: String = #function) {
    28→        logger.error("[RecordingLogger] [\(function)] \(message)", error: error)
    29→    }
    30→    
    31→    /// Logs a success message
    32→    /// - Parameters:
    33→    ///   - message: The success message to log
    34→    ///   - function: The function name (automatically captured via #function)
    35→    static func success(_ message: String, function: String = #function) {
    36→        logger.info("[RecordingLogger] ✅ [\(function)] \(message)")
    37→    }
    38→}

   247→    /// Sets up callbacks for when recordings are successfully transferred to iOS
   248→    private func setupTransferCallbacks() {

   250→        
   251→        // Listen for transfer completion via callback
   252→        connectivityManager.onTransferComplete = { [weak self] recordingId, success in
   253→            Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
   254→                guard let self = self else { return }
   255→
   256→                RecordingLogger.log("Transfer completion callback received for: \(recordingId), success: \(success)")
   257→
   258→                if success {
   259→                    self.handleTransferCompleted(recordingId: recordingId)
   260→                } else {
   261→                    self.handleTransferFailed(recordingId: recordingId)
   262→                }
   263→            }
   264→        }
   265→        
   266→        // Note: Transfer progress is now per-recording, observed individually in transferToiOS
   267→        // The global transferState has been removed to support concurrent transfers
   268→    }

   270→    /// Sets up observer for iOS processing status updates
   271→    private func observeiOSStatusUpdates() {
   272→        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
   273→            self,
   274→            selector: #selector(handleiOSStatusUpdate(_:)),
   275→            name: .iosProcessingStatusUpdated,
   276→            object: nil
   277→        )
   278→        
   279→        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
   280→            self,
   281→            selector: #selector(handleTransferConfirmed(_:)),
   282→            name: .watchRecordingTransferConfirmed,
   283→            object: nil
   284→        )
   285→    }
   286→    
   287→    /// Handles iOS status update notifications
   288→    /// This method is idempotent - it checks terminal state before processing
   289→    @objc private func handleiOSStatusUpdate(_ notification: Notification) {
   290→        guard let recordingId = notification.userInfo?["recordingId"] as? UUID,
   291→              let status = notification.userInfo?["status"] as? String else {
   292→            return
   293→        }
   294→        
   295→        let meetingId = notification.userInfo?["meetingId"] as? String
   296→        let uploadProgress = notification.userInfo?["uploadProgress"] as? Double
   297→        
   298→        guard let localRepo = repository as? LocalRecordingRepository else {
   299→            return
   300→        }
   301→        
   302→        // CRITICAL: Check if recording still exists and is not finalized
   303→        // This prevents crashes from late/out-of-order callbacks
   304→        guard let entity = try? localRepo.fetchEntityById(recordingId) else {
   305→            RecordingLogger.log("⏭️ Ignoring iOS status update - recording not found: \(recordingId)")
   306→            return
   307→        }
   308→        
   309→        let currentStatus = WatchRecordingStatus(rawValue: entity.status) ?? .pending
   310→        
   311→        // Allow "Created" status even for terminal recordings (final confirmation)
   312→        // But ignore all other late status updates for finalized recordings
   313→        if currentStatus.isTerminal && status != "Created" {
   314→            RecordingLogger.log("⏭️ Ignoring iOS status update '\(status)' for finalized recording: \(recordingId)")
   315→            return
   316→        }
   317→        
   318→        do {
   319→            try localRepo.updateiOSProcessingStatus(
   320→                recordingId,
   321→                status: status,
   322→                progress: uploadProgress,
   323→                meetingId: meetingId
   324→            )
   325→            
   326→            let progressStr = uploadProgress.map { " (\(Int($0 * 100))%)" } ?? ""
   327→            RecordingLogger.log("Updated iOS status for \(recordingId): \(status)\(progressStr)")
   328→            
   329→            // UI will auto-update via Core Data observers
   330→        } catch {
   331→            RecordingLogger.error("Failed to update iOS status | Error: \(error.localizedDescription)", error: error)
   332→        }
   333→    }
   334→    
   335→    /// Handles transfer confirmation notifications
   336→    /// This method is idempotent - it checks terminal state before processing
   337→    @objc private func handleTransferConfirmed(_ notification: Notification) {
   338→        guard let recordingId = notification.userInfo?["recordingId"] as? UUID else {
   339→            return
   340→        }
   341→        
   342→        guard let localRepo = repository as? LocalRecordingRepository else {
   343→            return
   344→        }
   345→        
   346→        // CRITICAL: Check if recording still exists and is not already confirmed
   347→        // This prevents duplicate processing of the same confirmation
   348→        guard let entity = try? localRepo.fetchEntityById(recordingId) else {
   349→            RecordingLogger.log("⏭️ Ignoring confirmation - recording not found: \(recordingId)")
   350→            return
   351→        }
   352→        
   353→        let currentStatus = WatchRecordingStatus(rawValue: entity.status) ?? .pending
   354→        if currentStatus.isTerminal {
   355→            RecordingLogger.log("⏭️ Ignoring duplicate confirmation for finalized recording: \(recordingId)")
   356→            return
   357→        }
   358→        
   359→        do {
   360→            try localRepo.updateStatus(recordingId, status: .sent)
   361→            RecordingLogger.log("Recording confirmed by iOS: \(recordingId)")
   362→            
   363→            // UI will auto-update via Core Data observers
   364→        } catch {
   365→            RecordingLogger.error("Failed to update confirmed status | Error: \(error.localizedDescription)", error: error)
   366→        }
   367→    }
   368→    
   369→    /// Handles iOS confirmation that a recording was received.
   370→    /// Local recording is NOT deleted here — deletion only happens when iOS confirms
   371→    /// full processing (status = "Created"), handled by the health check / cleanup service.
   372→    private func handleTransferCompleted(recordingId: UUID) {
   373→        RecordingLogger.log("=== TRANSFER CONFIRMED BY iOS: \(recordingId) ===")
   374→        
   375→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
   376→            // Remove from active transfers
   377→            self.activeTransfers.remove(recordingId)
   378→            self.timedOutTransferIds.remove(recordingId)
   379→            if self.currentTransferRecordingId == recordingId {
   380→                self.currentTransferRecordingId = nil
   381→            }
   382→            
   383→            // Update transfer state
   384→            self.transferState = .completed
   385→            self.isTransferringToiOS = !self.activeTransfers.isEmpty
   386→            
   387→            // Decrement pending sync count
   388→            if self.pendingSyncCount > 0 {
   389→                self.pendingSyncCount -= 1
   390→            }
   391→            
   392→            // Stop stall detection when all transfers are done
   393→            if self.activeTransfers.isEmpty {
   394→                self.stopStallDetection()
   395→            }
   396→            
   397→            // Reset transfer state after a brief delay (only if no active transfers)
   398→            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 2.0) {
   399→                if self.transferState == .completed && self.activeTransfers.isEmpty {
   400→                    self.transferState = .idle
   401→                }
   402→            }
   403→            
   404→            // Start next queued transfer (if any) now that the active slot is free.
   405→            self.startNextQueuedTransferIfIdle()
   406→        }
   407→        
   408→        // DO NOT delete the local recording here.
   409→        // The recording stays in Core Data (status = .sent) until iOS confirms full processing
   410→        // (iosProcessingStatus = "Created"). The WatchRecordingCleanupService will handle
   411→        // safe deletion once the meeting has been created on the backend.
   412→        RecordingLogger.log("Recording \(recordingId) stays local until iOS confirms processing")
   413→        
   414→        // Haptic feedback for successful sync
   415→        WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.success)
   416→    }
   417→    
   418→    /// Handles failed transfer - keeps local copy
   419→    private func handleTransferFailed(recordingId: UUID) {
   420→        RecordingLogger.error("Transfer failed for recording: \(recordingId)")
   421→        
   422→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
   423→            // Remove from active transfers
   424→            self.activeTransfers.remove(recordingId)
   425→            if self.currentTransferRecordingId == recordingId {
   426→                self.currentTransferRecordingId = nil
   427→            }
   428→            
   429→            // Update legacy state
   430→            self.transferState = .failed("Transfer failed")
   431→            self.isTransferringToiOS = !self.activeTransfers.isEmpty
   432→            
   433→            // Reset transfer state after showing error briefly (only if no active transfers)
   434→            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 3.0) {
   435→                if case .failed(_) = self.transferState, self.activeTransfers.isEmpty {
   436→                    self.transferState = .idle
   437→                }
   438→            }
   439→            
   440→            // Continue queue drain by attempting the next pending recording.
   441→            self.startNextQueuedTransferIfIdle()
   442→        }
   443→        
   444→        // Recording stays local - will be retried later
   445→        WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.failure)
   446→    }

   504→    // MARK: - Keep-Alive Session Management
   505→    
   506→    /// Starts an ExtendedRuntimeSession to keep the app alive during recording.
   507→    /// Provides ~1 hour of background execution. User interaction (EXTEND/alerts)
   508→    /// keeps the app in the foreground, extending effective recording duration.
   509→    private func startExtendedRuntimeSessionFallback() {
   510→        let sessionStarted = extendedRuntimeSessionManager.startSession(
   511→            onStateChange: { sessionState in
   512→                RecordingLogger.log("Extended runtime session state changed: \(sessionState)")
   513→            },
   514→            onExpiration: { [weak self] in
   515→                Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
   516→                    self?.handleExtendedRuntimeSessionExpiration()
   517→                }
   518→            },
   519→            onInvalidation: { [weak self] reason, error in
   520→                Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
   521→                    self?.handleExtendedRuntimeSessionInvalidation(reason: reason, error: error)
   522→                }
   523→            }
   524→        )
   525→        
   526→        if sessionStarted {
   527→            RecordingLogger.success("ExtendedRuntimeSession fallback started (~1 hour limit)")
   528→        } else {
   529→            RecordingLogger.log("⚠️ ExtendedRuntimeSession fallback could not start")
   530→        }
   531→    }

   535→        extendedRuntimeSessionManager.stopSession()

   550→    // MARK: - Graceful Recording Shutdown
   551→    
   552→    /// Performs a graceful shutdown of an active recording: saves chunks, initiates transfer,
   553→    /// logs the reason to Sentry (with user context), and shows a user-friendly message.
   554→    ///
   555→    /// Every error path that interrupts a recording must funnel through this method so that
   556→    /// no recorded audio is lost.
   557→    ///
   558→    /// - Parameters:
   559→    ///   - reason: Human-readable reason shown in the UI (e.g. "Session time limit was reached.")
   560→    ///   - sentryMessage: Technical message sent to Sentry for debugging / alerting
   561→    ///   - error: Optional underlying error object attached to the Sentry event
   562→    private func gracefulRecordingShutdown(
   563→        reason: String,
   564→        sentryMessage: String,
   565→        error: Error? = nil
   566→    ) {
   567→        guard state == .recording || state == .paused else { return }
   568→        
   569→        DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   570→            guard let self = self, self.state == .recording || self.state == .paused else { return }
   571→            
   572→            let recordedDuration = self.elapsed
   573→            
   574→            // Save the recording and initiate transfer to iPhone
   575→            self.stopRecording()
   576→            
   577→            // Log to Sentry so we can track which users hit this and why
   578→            RecordingLogger.error(
   579→                "\(sentryMessage) — recording gracefully stopped after \(Int(recordedDuration))s",
   580→                error: error
   581→            )
   582→            
   583→            self.state = .error("Meeting saved. \(reason)")
   584→            WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.notification)
   585→        }
   586→    }
   587→    
   588→    /// Handles extended runtime session expiration
   589→    /// Called when the session is about to expire (typically after ~4 hours).
   590→    private func handleExtendedRuntimeSessionExpiration() {
   591→        RecordingLogger.log("⚠️ Extended runtime session is expiring")
   592→        
   593→        gracefulRecordingShutdown(
   594→            reason: "Session time limit was reached.",
   595→            sentryMessage: "Session time limit reached (expiration callback)"
   596→        )
   597→    }
   598→    
   599→    /// Handles extended runtime session invalidation
   600→    /// Called when the session is invalidated for various reasons
   601→    /// - Parameters:
   602→    ///   - reason: The reason for invalidation
   603→    ///   - error: Optional error if invalidation was due to an error
   604→    private func handleExtendedRuntimeSessionInvalidation(
   605→        reason: WKExtendedRuntimeSessionInvalidationReason,
   606→        error: Error?
   607→    ) {
   608→        RecordingLogger.log("Extended runtime session invalidated - Reason: \(reason)")
   609→        
   610→        switch reason {
   611→        case .none:
   612→            // Manual invalidation (expected when stopping recording) — no action needed
   613→            RecordingLogger.log("Session invalidated manually (expected)")
   614→            
   615→        case .sessionInProgress:
   616→            RecordingLogger.log("Session invalidated - another session already in progress")
   617→            gracefulRecordingShutdown(
   618→                reason: "A session conflict occurred.",
   619→                sentryMessage: "Session invalidated — sessionInProgress"
   620→            )
   621→            
   622→        case .expired:

   630→            RecordingLogger.log("Session invalidated - app resigned frontmost status (will not pause during ringing)")

   637→        case .suppressedBySystem:
   638→            RecordingLogger.log("Session invalidated - suppressed by system")

   640→                reason: "A system limitation occurred.",
   641→                sentryMessage: "Session invalidated — suppressedBySystem"
   642→            )
   643→            
   644→        case .error:
   645→            RecordingLogger.log("Session invalidated due to error")

   647→                reason: "An unexpected error occurred.",
   648→                sentryMessage: "Session invalidated — error",
   649→                error: error
   650→            )

   661→    // MARK: - Recording State Resync

   663→    /// Observes iPhone's request to re-send our current recording state.

   665→    /// contain a stale `isRecordingOnWatch = true`. The iPhone sends a message asking us to confirm,
   666→    /// and we respond with our actual current state via application context.

   670→            selector: #selector(handleRecordingStateResyncRequest),

   677→    /// Observes iPhone's request to retry/resend a specific recording transfer.

   680→            self,
   681→            selector: #selector(handleTransferRetryRequestFromiPhone(_:)),
