   686→    }
   687→    
   688→    @objc private func handleRecordingStateResyncRequest() {
   689→        RecordingLogger.log("Received recording state resync request from iPhone")
   690→        DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   691→            guard let self = self else { return }
   692→            let isCurrentlyRecording = (self.state == .recording || self.state == .paused)
   693→            let currentStartDate = isCurrentlyRecording ? self.startDate : nil
   694→            
   695→            RecordingLogger.log("Re-sending recording state: isRecording=\(isCurrentlyRecording), startedAt=\(String(describing: currentStartDate))")
   696→            WatchConnectivityManager.shared.sendRecordingOnWatchIndicator(
   697→                isRecording: isCurrentlyRecording,
   698→                startedAt: currentStartDate
   699→            )
   700→        }
   701→    }
   702→    
   703→    @objc private func handleTransferRetryRequestFromiPhone(_ notification: Notification) {
   704→        guard let userInfo = notification.userInfo,
   705→              let recordingIdString = userInfo[WatchMessageKey.recordingId] as? String,
   706→              let recordingId = UUID(uuidString: recordingIdString) else {
   707→            RecordingLogger.log("Received invalid retry request payload from iPhone")
   708→            return
   709→        }
   710→        
   711→        RecordingLogger.log("Received retry request from iPhone for recording: \(recordingId)")
   712→        DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   713→            self?.handleRetryRequestFromiPhone(recordingId: recordingId)
   714→        }
   715→    }
   716→    
   717→    /// Handles iPhone-initiated retry while respecting one-recording-at-a-time queueing.
   718→    private func handleRetryRequestFromiPhone(recordingId: UUID) {
   719→        guard let localRepo = repository as? LocalRecordingRepository else {
   720→            RecordingLogger.error("Cannot handle iPhone retry request - repository is not LocalRecordingRepository")
   721→            return
   722→        }
   723→        
   724→        guard let recording = try? repository.fetchAllRecordings().first(where: { $0.id == recordingId }) else {
   725→            RecordingLogger.log("Retry request ignored - recording not found locally: \(recordingId)")
   726→            return
   727→        }
   728→        
   729→        if currentTransferRecordingId == recordingId || activeTransfers.contains(recordingId) ||
   730→            connectivityManager.isRecordingTransferring(recordingId) {
   731→            RecordingLogger.log("Retry request ignored because recording is already transferring: \(recordingId)")
   732→            return
   733→        }
   734→        
   735→        // If currently busy with a different recording, queue this one as pending.
   736→        if let activeId = currentTransferRecordingId, activeId != recordingId {
   737→            do {
   738→                try localRepo.updateStatus(recordingId, status: .pending, error: "Queued from iPhone retry request")
   739→                RecordingLogger.log("Queued retry request while transfer slot busy (\(activeId)) for recording: \(recordingId)")
   740→            } catch {
   741→                RecordingLogger.error("Failed to mark recording as queued from iPhone retry request", error: error)
   742→            }
   743→            return
   744→        }
   745→        
   746→        if activeTransfers.contains(where: { $0 != recordingId }) {
   747→            do {
   748→                try localRepo.updateStatus(recordingId, status: .pending, error: "Queued from iPhone retry request")
   749→                RecordingLogger.log("Queued retry request while another active transfer exists for recording: \(recordingId)")
   750→            } catch {
   751→                RecordingLogger.error("Failed to mark recording as queued from iPhone retry request", error: error)
   752→            }
   753→            return
   754→        }
   755→        
   756→        // Ensure request retries are sendable through normal pipeline.
   757→        do {
   758→            try localRepo.updateStatus(recordingId, status: .pending, error: "Retry requested by iPhone")
   759→        } catch {
   760→            RecordingLogger.error("Failed to reset status before iPhone-requested retry", error: error)
   761→        }
   762→        
   763→        transferToiOS(recording: recording)
   764→    }

  2128→        let phaseRaw = context[WatchContextKey.transferredRecordingAckPhase] as? String

  2133→

  2194→                // ========================================

  2253→    /// Removes a confirmed recording's progress and metadata entries from

  2258→    private func removeConfirmedRecordingFromContext(recordingId: UUID) {

  2265→        if var progressUpdates = context[WatchContextKey.transferProgressUpdates] as? [String: Double] {

  2267→                if progressUpdates.isEmpty {
  2268→                    context.removeValue(forKey: WatchContextKey.transferProgressUpdates)
  2269→                } else {
  2270→                    context[WatchContextKey.transferProgressUpdates] = progressUpdates
  2271→                }
  2272→                didChange = true
  2273→            }
  2274→        }
  2275→        
  2276→        if var metadataDict = context[WatchContextKey.transferMetadata] as? [String: [String: Any]] {

  2279→                    context.removeValue(forKey: WatchContextKey.transferMetadata)
  2280→                } else {
  2281→                    context[WatchContextKey.transferMetadata] = metadataDict
  2282→                }

  2284→            }

  2329→    /// Sends confirmation to the watch that recordings have been received

  2336→    @discardableResult
  2337→    func confirmRecordingsReceived(
  2338→        _ recordingIds: [UUID],
  2339→        phase: RecordingTransferConfirmationPhase = .received
  2340→    ) -> Bool {
  2341→        guard !recordingIds.isEmpty else { return true }
  2342→        
  2343→        // Dispatch to main thread if not already there to avoid deadlock risk
  2344→        if Thread.isMainThread {
  2345→            return performConfirmRecordingsReceived(recordingIds: recordingIds, phase: phase)
  2346→        } else {
  2347→            DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
  2348→                self?.performConfirmRecordingsReceived(recordingIds: recordingIds, phase: phase)
  2349→            }
  2350→            // Async path can't report back; assume queued (callers on main thread should use sync path)
  2351→            return true
  2352→        }
  2353→    }

  2357→    /// Unlike applicationContext (which is last-write-wins and can be overwritten by auth
  2358→    /// token syncs or status updates), transferUserInfo is queued -- every call is
  2359→    /// guaranteed to be delivered even if multiple calls happen in rapid succession.
  2360→    @discardableResult
  2361→    private func performConfirmRecordingsReceived(
  2362→        recordingIds: [UUID],
  2363→        phase: RecordingTransferConfirmationPhase

  2365→        dispatchPrecondition(condition: .onQueue(.main))
  2366→        
  2367→        guard let session = session, activationState == .activated else {
  2368→            logger.warning("[WatchConnectivity] Cannot send confirmation - session not active. Will retry later.")

  2374→        // Use transferUserInfo for queued, guaranteed delivery of confirmations.

  2376→        // updates (e.g., auth token syncs, progress updates, status changes).
  2377→        let userInfo: [String: Any] = [

  2381→        
  2382→        session.transferUserInfo(userInfo)
  2383→        logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Sent \(phase.rawValue) confirmation via transferUserInfo for \(recordingIds.count) recording(s)")
  2384→        return true
  2385→    }

  2388→    /// Note: With Core Data, this should be handled by clearing RecordingMetadata in repository
  2389→    func clearConfirmedRecordings() {
  2390→        logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] clearConfirmedRecordings called - Core Data cleanup should be used instead")
  2391→        // No-op - Core Data is the source of truth now
  2392→    }

2870→    /// IMPORTANT: The file at file.fileURL is deleted immediately after this method returns!
  2871→    /// We must copy it synchronously before returning.
  2872→    func session(_ session: WCSession, didReceive file: WCSessionFile) {
  2873→        // CRITICAL: Copy file IMMEDIATELY before this method returns
  2874→        // WatchConnectivity deletes the temp file right after the delegate callback
  2875→        
  2876→        // Validate metadata exists
  2877→        guard let metadataDict = file.metadata else {
  2878→            logger.fatal("[WatchConnectivity] ❌ CRITICAL DATA LOSS: Received file without metadata - file will be deleted!")
  2879→            logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] File URL: \(file.fileURL.path)", error: nil)
  2880→            // Log file info if possible before it's deleted
  2881→            if let attrs = try? FileManager.default.attributesOfItem(atPath: file.fileURL.path),
  2882→               let size = attrs[.size] as? Int64 {
  2883→                logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] File size was: \(ByteCountFormatter.string(fromByteCount: size, countStyle: .file))", error: nil)
  2884→            }
  2885→            return
  2886→        }
  2887→        
  2888→        guard let metadata = RecordingTransferMetadata.fromDictionary(metadataDict) else {
  2889→            logger.fatal("[WatchConnectivity] ❌ CRITICAL DATA LOSS: Invalid metadata for received file - file will be deleted!")
  2890→            logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] File URL: \(file.fileURL.path)", error: nil)
  2891→            logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] Metadata dict: \(metadataDict)", error: nil)
  2892→            // Log file info if possible before it's deleted
  2893→            if let attrs = try? FileManager.default.attributesOfItem(atPath: file.fileURL.path),
  2894→               let size = attrs[.size] as? Int64 {
  2895→                logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] File size was: \(ByteCountFormatter.string(fromByteCount: size, countStyle: .file))", error: nil)
  2896→            }
  2897→            return
  2898→        }
  2899→        
  2900→        let recordingId = metadata.id
  2901→        let isChunkedTransfer = metadata.isChunked
  2902→        
  2903→        logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] FILE RECEIVED FROM WATCH — Recording ID: \(recordingId), Filename: \(metadata.filename), isChunked: \(isChunkedTransfer)")
  2904→        if let chunkInfo = metadata.chunkInfo {
  2905→            logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Chunk details — index: \(chunkInfo.chunkIndex), isLast: \(chunkInfo.isLastChunk), parentId: \(chunkInfo.parentRecordingId), chunkDuration: \(chunkInfo.chunkDuration)s, totalSoFar: \(chunkInfo.totalDurationSoFar)s")
  2906→        }
  2907→        
  2908→        // ========================================
  2909→        // DUPLICATE DETECTION - Simplified Logic
  2910→        // ========================================
  2911→        // For chunked transfers, skip duplicate detection (chunks are always accepted;
  2912→        // ChunkAssemblyManager handles deduplication at the chunk level).
  2913→        // For single-file transfers, block if already completed or actively processing.
  2914→        // ========================================
  2915→        
  2916→        if isChunkedTransfer {
  2917→            logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Chunked transfer — skipping duplicate detection (handled by ChunkAssemblyManager)")
  2918→        } else {
  2919→        logger.debug("[WatchConnectivity] Checking for duplicates...")
  2920→        
  2921→        // metadataRepository uses viewContext (main-queue NSManagedObjectContext) but
  2922→        // session(_:didReceive file:) runs on WCSession's background queue.
  2923→        // Access Core Data on the main thread to avoid threading violations.
  2924→        enum DuplicateCheckResult {
  2925→            case alreadyProcessed
  2926→            case currentlyProcessing
  2927→            case allowRetry
  2928→            case noExistingRecord
  2929→        }
  2930→        
  2931→        let checkResult: DuplicateCheckResult = DispatchQueue.main.sync {
  2932→            do {
  2933→                guard let existing = try metadataRepository?.fetchById(recordingId) else {
  2934→                    return .noExistingRecord
  2935→                }
  2936→                let status = existing.statusEnum
  2937→                if status == .created || existing.meetingId != nil {
  2938→                    return .alreadyProcessed
  2939→                }
  2940→                if status == .uploading || status == .creatingMeeting {
  2941→                    return .currentlyProcessing
  2942→                }
  2943→                if status.isFailed {
  2944→                    return .allowRetry
  2945→                }
  2946→                return .noExistingRecord
  2947→            } catch {
  2948→                logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] Error checking for duplicate: \(error)")
  2949→                return .noExistingRecord
  2950→            }
  2951→        }
  2952→        
  2953→        switch checkResult {
  2954→        case .alreadyProcessed:
  2955→            logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Recording already processed: \(recordingId)")
  2956→            DispatchQueue.main.async {
  2957→                self.incomingTransferProgress[recordingId] = .completed
  2958→                self.updateCachedAggregateStates()
  2959→                self.confirmRecordingsReceived([recordingId])
  2960→            }
  2961→            return
  2962→            
  2963→        case .currentlyProcessing:
  2964→            logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Recording currently processing: \(recordingId)")
  2965→            DispatchQueue.main.async {
  2966→                self.incomingTransferProgress[recordingId] = .receiving(progress: 1.0)
  2967→                self.updateCachedAggregateStates()
  2968→                _ = self.confirmRecordingsReceived([recordingId])
  2969→            }
  2970→            return
  2971→            
  2972→        case .allowRetry:
  2973→            logger.info("[WatchConnectivity] Retrying failed recording: \(recordingId)")
  2974→            
  2975→        case .noExistingRecord:
  2976→            logger.debug("🔵 [WatchConnectivity] ✅ New or retryable recording - proceeding")
  2977→        }
  2978→        
  2979→        // ========================================
  2980→        // DUPLICATE DETECTION - END
  2981→        // ========================================
  2982→        } // end if !isChunkedTransfer
  2983→        
  2984→        // Update transfer state to "receiving" when file arrives (before we process it)
  2985→        // For chunks, use parentRecordingId so all chunks show under one entry
  2986→        // Remove any placeholder pending states since we now have the actual recording
  2987→        let progressTrackingId = metadata.chunkInfo?.parentRecordingId ?? recordingId
  2988→        
  2989→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
  2990→            // Get current progress from watch if available (from progress updates)
  2991→            var initialProgress: Double? = nil
  2992→            if let currentState = self.incomingTransferProgress[progressTrackingId],
  2993→               case .receiving(let progress) = currentState {
  2994→                initialProgress = progress
  2995→            }
  2996→            
  2997→            // Always remove placeholder when a real transfer arrives
  2998→            if self.incomingTransferProgress[Self.pendingPlaceholderID] != nil {
  2999→                self.incomingTransferProgress.removeValue(forKey: Self.pendingPlaceholderID)
  3000→                self.logger.debug("[WatchConnectivity] Removed placeholder - real transfer arrived: \(progressTrackingId)")
  3001→            }
  3002→            
  3003→            // Clean up any old completed or 100% transfers when a new transfer arrives
  3004→            // This prevents accumulation of completed/finished transfers in the UI
  3005→            let transfersToRemove = self.incomingTransferProgress.keys.filter { id in
  3006→                if id == Self.pendingPlaceholderID { return false }
  3007→                if id == progressTrackingId { return false } // Don't remove the new one
  3008→                
  3009→                // Remove completed transfers
  3010→                if case .completed = self.incomingTransferProgress[id] { return true }
  3011→                
  3012→                // Also remove transfers that are at 100% progress (waiting for file)
  3013→                // These show "Receiving 100%" but file hasn't been received yet
  3014→                return false
  3015→            }
  3016→            
  3017→            if !transfersToRemove.isEmpty {
  3018→                for id in transfersToRemove {
  3019→                    self.incomingTransferProgress.removeValue(forKey: id)
  3020→                    self.logger.debug("[WatchConnectivity] Cleaned up old completed transfer: \(id)")
  3021→                }
  3022→                self.logger.debug("[WatchConnectivity] Cleaned up \(transfersToRemove.count) old transfer(s)")
  3023→                // Explicitly update state after cleanup to ensure UI updates
  3024→                self.updateCachedAggregateStates()
  3025→            }
  3026→            
  3027→            // Set state to receiving for this actual recording (preserve progress if we have it)
  3028→            self.suppressedStaleIncomingProgressIds.remove(progressTrackingId)
  3029→            self.suppressedStaleIncomingProgressIds.remove(recordingId)
  3030→            self.incomingProgressLastChangeAt[progressTrackingId] = Date()
  3031→            if let initialProgress {
  3032→                self.incomingProgressLastValue[progressTrackingId] = initialProgress
  3033→            }
  3034→            self.incomingTransferProgress[progressTrackingId] = .receiving(progress: initialProgress)
  3035→            self.updateCachedAggregateStates()
  3036→        }
  3037→        
  3038→        let originalURL = file.fileURL
  3039→        
  3040→        // ========================================
  3041→        // LOW-STORAGE PROTECTION
  3042→        // Check available disk space BEFORE copying to prevent data loss.
  3043→        // If the copy fails due to insufficient storage, watchOS deletes
  3044→        // the original temp file and the recording is permanently lost.
  3045→        // By checking first, we can skip the copy and let the Watch
  3046→        // keep its local copy for a later retry.
  3047→        // ========================================
  3048→        do {
  3049→            let incomingFileSize: Int64
  3050→            if let attrs = try? FileManager.default.attributesOfItem(atPath: originalURL.path),
  3051→               let size = attrs[.size] as? Int64 {
  3052→                incomingFileSize = size
  3053→            } else {
  3054→                incomingFileSize = 0
  3055→            }
  3056→            
  3057→            let tempDir = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory
  3058→            let fsAttributes = try FileManager.default.attributesOfFileSystem(forPath: tempDir.path)
  3059→            let freeSpace = fsAttributes[.systemFreeSize] as? Int64 ?? 0
  3060→            
  3061→            // Require at least 2x the file size to be safe (copy + processing headroom)
  3062→            let requiredSpace = incomingFileSize * 2
  3063→            
  3064→            if freeSpace < requiredSpace && incomingFileSize > 0 {
  3065→                let freeSpaceMB = freeSpace / (1024 * 1024)
  3066→                let requiredMB = requiredSpace / (1024 * 1024)
  3067→                logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] INSUFFICIENT STORAGE: \(freeSpaceMB) MB free, need \(requiredMB) MB", error: nil)
  3068→                logger.error("[WatchConnectivity] Skipping file copy to protect Watch recording. Watch will retain its copy.", error: nil)
  3069→

800→    private func handleAudioInterruption(notification: Notification) {
   801→        guard let userInfo = notification.userInfo,
   802→              let typeValue = userInfo[AVAudioSessionInterruptionTypeKey] as? UInt,
   803→              let type = AVAudioSession.InterruptionType(rawValue: typeValue) else {
   804→            return
   805→        }
   806→        
   807→        switch type {
   808→        case .began:
   809→            RecordingLogger.log("Audio interruption began (e.g., phone call) — deferring pause by \(interruptionDeferralSeconds)s to avoid ring-only pauses")
   810→
   811→            // Cancel any existing pending pause
   812→            pendingInterruptionPauseWorkItem?.cancel()
   813→
   814→            // Only consider pausing if we're actively recording; defer to see if the interruption clears quickly
   815→            guard self.state == .recording else { return }
   816→
   817→            let workItem = DispatchWorkItem { [weak self] in
   818→                guard let self = self else { return }
   819→                // If still recording after the deferral window, treat as a real/connected interruption and pause
   820→                if self.state == .recording {
   821→                    self.pauseRecording(reason: .audioInterruption)
   822→                }
   823→            }
   824→            self.pendingInterruptionPauseWorkItem = workItem
   825→
   826→            // If options indicate "shouldResume" or no options provided, treat as potentially transient (ring-only) and keep deferring.
   827→            if let optionsValue = userInfo[AVAudioSessionInterruptionOptionKey] as? UInt {
   828→                let options = AVAudioSession.InterruptionOptions(rawValue: optionsValue)
   829→                if options.contains(.shouldResume) {
   830→                    // Likely ring-only or transient; proceed with deferral without immediate pause.
   831→                }
   832→            }
   833→
   834→            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + interruptionDeferralSeconds, execute: workItem)
   835→
   836→        case .ended:
   837→            RecordingLogger.log("Audio interruption ended — cancelling any deferred pause and attempting resume if needed")
   838→
   839→            // Cancel the deferred pause (ring-only interruptions end quickly and should not pause)
   840→            pendingInterruptionPauseWorkItem?.cancel()
   841→            pendingInterruptionPauseWorkItem = nil
   842→
   843→            // If we never transitioned to paused but the underlying recorder might have been halted by the OS,
   844→            // attempt a best-effort resume of the recording engine.
   845→            if self.state == .recording {
   846→                self.chunkedRecordingManager.resumeRecording()
   847→            }
   848→
   849→            // Respect existing auto-resume behavior when we did pause due to an interruption
   850→            if let optionsValue = userInfo[AVAudioSessionInterruptionOptionKey] as? UInt {
   851→                let options = AVAudioSession.InterruptionOptions(rawValue: optionsValue)
   852→                if options.contains(.shouldResume) {
   853→                    handleAutoResume()
   854→                }
   855→            } else {
   856→                // If no options provided, try to auto-resume if it was an interruption pause
   857→                handleAutoResume()
   858→            }
   859→        @unknown default:
   860→            RecordingLogger.log("Unknown interruption type: \(typeValue)")
   861→        }
   862→    }
   863→    
   864→    /// Checks if recording should auto-resume and resumes if appropriate
   865→    /// Only auto-resumes if the pause was due to an audio interruption (not manual)
   866→    func handleAutoResume() {
   867→        guard state == .paused else {
   868→            return
   869→        }
   870→        // Never stop due to interruptions; only resume if it was an interruption pause.
   871→        
   872→        // Only auto-resume if it was paused due to audio interruption
   873→        if pauseReason == .audioInterruption {
   874→            RecordingLogger.log("Auto-resuming recording after audio interruption ended")
   875→            resumeRecording()
   876→        } else {
   877→            RecordingLogger.log("Not auto-resuming - pause was manual or unknown reason")
   878→        }
   879→    }

   881→    // MARK: - Recording Controls
   882→
   883→    func startRecording() {
   884→        RecordingLogger.log("=== START RECORDING ===")
   885→        
   886→        // Start extended runtime session to keep the app alive in the background.
   887→        // The session provides ~1 hour of background execution. Since the user
   888→        // periodically interacts (EXTEND button, dismiss alerts), the app stays
   889→        // foreground-active, mitigating this limit for longer recordings.
   890→        startExtendedRuntimeSessionFallback()
   891→        
   892→        // Continue with recording setup immediately
   893→        
   894→        // Update state immediately for instant UI feedback
   895→        // This prevents the UI from freezing while audio session setup occurs
   896→        state = .recording
   897→        
   898→        // Setup audio session (this can block, but state is already updated)
   899→        guard setupAudioSession() else {
   900→            RecordingLogger.error("Cannot start recording - audio session setup failed")
   901→            // Clean up keep-alive session if audio setup fails
   902→            stopKeepAliveSession()
   903→            state = .error("Audio session setup failed")
   904→            WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.failure)
   905→            return
   906→        }
   907→        
   908→        // Start recording via ChunkedRecordingManager.
   909→        // The manager handles AVAudioRecorder lifecycle, countdown timer, and chunk creation.
   910→        // Chunks are created when user presses EXTEND; completed chunks are stored locally.
   911→        // ALL chunks are transferred to iPhone only after recording stops (in stopRecording).
   912→        let recordingId = chunkedRecordingManager.startRecording(
   913→            onChunkCompleted: { chunkURL, chunkMetadata in
   914→                let chunkIndex = chunkMetadata.chunkInfo?.chunkIndex ?? -1
   915→                let isLast = chunkMetadata.chunkInfo?.isLastChunk ?? false
   916→                RecordingLogger.log("Chunk \(chunkIndex) saved locally (isLast: \(isLast)), will transfer after stop")
   917→            },
   918→            onRecordingFinished: {
   919→                RecordingLogger.log("All chunks finalized — ready for transfer")
   920→            }
   921→        )
   922→        
   923→        guard let recordingId = recordingId else {
   924→            RecordingLogger.error("ChunkedRecordingManager failed to start recording")
   925→            stopKeepAliveSession()
   926→            state = .error("Failed to start recording")
   927→            WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.failure)
   928→            return
   929→        }
   930→        
   931→        currentRecordingId = recordingId
   932→        
   933→        // No separate currentRecordingURL needed -- ChunkedRecordingManager manages chunk files
   934→        // currentRecordingURL is set to nil; stopRecording will use the manager's output
   935→        currentRecordingURL = nil
   936→        
   937→        RecordingLogger.log("Recording ID: \(recordingId)")
   938→        RecordingLogger.log("Recording started via ChunkedRecordingManager (dynamic chunk allocation)")
   939→        
   940→        // Recording started successfully via ChunkedRecordingManager
   941→        let now = Date()
   942→        startDate = now
   943→        recordingStartTime = now  // Capture the original start time (not affected by pause/resume)
   944→        accumulated = 0
   945→        elapsed = 0
   946→        pauseReason = nil  // Clear any previous pause reason
   947→        state = .recording
   948→        startTimer()
   949→        
   950→        RecordingLogger.success("Recording started successfully!")
   951→        RecordingLogger.log("  Recording ID: \(recordingId)")
   952→        RecordingLogger.log("  Start Time: \(now)")
   953→        
   954→        WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.start)
   955→        
   956→        // Notify iPhone so it can show Live Activity and notification (Guideline 2.5.14)
   957→        WatchConnectivityManager.shared.sendRecordingOnWatchIndicator(isRecording: true, startedAt: startDate)
   958→    }
   959→
   960→    func pauseRecording(reason: PauseReason = .manual) {
   961→        RecordingLogger.log("=== PAUSE RECORDING (reason: \(reason)) ===")
   962→        
   963→        guard state == .recording else {
   964→            RecordingLogger.log("Cannot pause - not in recording state (current: \(state))")
   965→            return
   966→        }
   967→        
   968→        // Pause via ChunkedRecordingManager (pauses the underlying AVAudioRecorder)
   969→        chunkedRecordingManager.pauseRecording()
   970→        
   971→        // Also pause direct recorder reference if still set (legacy compatibility)
   972→        audioRecorder?.pause()
   973→        RecordingLogger.log("Audio recorder paused")
   974→        
   975→        stopTimer()
   976→        if let s = startDate {
   977→            accumulated += Date().timeIntervalSince(s)
   978→        }
   979→        startDate = nil
   980→        pauseReason = reason
   981→        state = .paused
   982→        
   983→        RecordingLogger.success("Recording paused. Accumulated time: \(accumulated)s, Reason: \(reason)")
   984→        WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.stop)
   985→    }
   986→
   987→    func resumeRecording() {
   988→        RecordingLogger.log("=== RESUME RECORDING ===")
   989→        
   990→        guard state == .paused else {
   991→            RecordingLogger.log("Cannot resume - not in paused state (current: \(state))")
   992→            return
   993→        }
   994→        
   995→        // Resume via ChunkedRecordingManager (resumes the underlying AVAudioRecorder)
   996→        chunkedRecordingManager.resumeRecording()
   997→        
   998→        // Also resume direct recorder reference if still set (legacy compatibility)
   999→        let resumed = audioRecorder?.record() ?? false
  1000→        RecordingLogger.log("Audio recorder resumed: \(resumed)")
  1001→        
  1002→        startDate = Date()
  1003→        pauseReason = nil  // Clear pause reason when resuming
  1004→        state = .recording
  1005→        startTimer()
  1006→        
  1007→        RecordingLogger.success("Recording resumed")
  1008→        WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.start)
  1009→    }
  1010→
  1011→    // MARK: - Extend Recording
  1012→    
  1013→    /// Extends the recording by 45 minutes.
  1014→    /// Creates a chunk boundary (finalizes current chunk, starts new one),
  1015→    /// adds time to the countdown, and starts a fresh ExtendedRuntimeSession
  1016→    /// so the OS grants another ~1 hour of background execution.
  1017→    func extendRecording() {
  1018→        RecordingLogger.log("=== EXTEND RECORDING ===")
  1019→        
  1020→        guard state == .recording else {
  1021→            RecordingLogger.log("Cannot extend - not in recording state (current: \(state))")
  1022→            return
  1023→        }
  1024→        
  1025→        let success = chunkedRecordingManager.extendRecording()
  1026→        
  1027→        if success {
  1028→            RecordingLogger.success("Recording extended by \(chunkedRecordingManager.configuredExtendAmountSeconds / 60) minutes")
  1029→            
  1030→            // Reset alert tracking so alerts fire again for the new countdown window
  1031→            shownAlerts.removeAll()
  1032→            activeAlert = nil
  1033→            
  1034→            // Start a fresh ExtendedRuntimeSession to get another ~1 hour of background time.
  1035→            // startSession() internally parks the old session and invalidates it safely.
  1036→            RecordingLogger.log("Renewing ExtendedRuntimeSession for extended recording...")
  1037→            startExtendedRuntimeSessionFallback()
  1038→            
  1039→            WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.success)
  1040→        } else {
  1041→            RecordingLogger.error("Failed to extend recording")
  1042→            WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.failure)
  1043→        }
  1044→    }
  1045→    
  1046→    /// Dismisses the currently active time-remaining alert
  1047→    func dismissAlert() {
  1048→        activeAlert = nil
  1049→    }
  1050→    
  1051→    // MARK: - Alert Threshold Checking
  1052→    
  1053→    /// Checks if the remaining time has crossed any alert thresholds
  1054→    /// and triggers the appropriate alert with haptic feedback.
  1055→    /// Called every time remainingTime is updated.
  1056→    /// - Parameter remaining: Current remaining time in seconds
  1057→    private func checkAlertThresholds(remaining: TimeInterval) {
  1058→        guard state == .recording else { return }
  1059→        
  1060→        for threshold in alertThresholds {
  1061→            // Fire when remaining dips at or below the threshold, but only once per threshold
  1062→            if remaining <= threshold && remaining > 0 && !shownAlerts.contains(threshold) {
  1063→                shownAlerts.insert(threshold)
  1064→                
  1065→                // Determine which alert to show
  1066→                let alert: RecordingAlert
  1067→                switch threshold {
  1068→                case let t where t == 5 * 60:
  1069→                    alert = .fiveMinutes
  1070→                case let t where t == 2 * 60:
  1071→                    alert = .twoMinutes
  1072→                case let t where t == 30:
  1073→                    alert = .thirtySeconds
  1074→                default:
  1075→                    continue
  1076→                }
  1077→                
  1078→                RecordingLogger.log("Alert triggered: \(alert) (remaining: \(remaining)s)")
  1079→                
  1080→                // Show the alert and play haptic
  1081→                activeAlert = alert
  1082→                WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.notification)
  1083→                
  1084→                // Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds
  1085→                DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 5.0) { [weak self] in
  1086→                    if self?.activeAlert == alert {
  1087→                        self?.activeAlert = nil
  1088→                    }
  1089→                }
  1090→                
  1091→                // Only show one alert at a time (the most urgent)
  1092→                break
  1093→            }
  1094→        }
  1095→    }
  1096→    
  1097→    // MARK: - Stall Detection & Retry
  1098→    
  1099→    /// Starts monitoring transfer progress for stalls (works for both chunked and single-file).
  1100→    /// Call after transfers begin.
  1101→    func startStallDetection() {
  1102→        RecordingLogger.log("Starting stall detection timer")
  1103→        stallCheckTimer?.invalidate()
  1104→        
  1105→        // Seed single-file progress tracking for every active transfer
  1106→        let now = Date()
  1107→        for recordingId in activeTransfers {
  1108→            if recordingLastProgressUpdate[recordingId] == nil {
  1109→                recordingLastProgressUpdate[recordingId] = now
  1110→                recordingLastProgressValue[recordingId] = 0.0
  1111→            }
  1112→        }
  1113→        
  1114→        stallCheckTimer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: 30, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
  1115→            Task { @MainActor in
  1116→                self?.checkForStalledTransfers()
  1117→            }
  1118→        }
  1119→    }
  1120→    
  1121→    /// Stops the stall detection timer and clears all progress tracking state
  1122→    private func stopStallDetection() {
  1123→        stallCheckTimer?.invalidate()
  1124→        stallCheckTimer = nil
  1125→        chunkLastProgressUpdate.removeAll()
  1126→        chunkTransferProgress.removeAll()
  1127→        recordingLastProgressUpdate.removeAll()
  1128→        recordingLastProgressValue.removeAll()
  1129→        retryStartTime = nil
  1130→    }
  1131→    
  1132→    /// Updates progress for a specific chunk (called from transfer progress callbacks)
  1133→    /// - Parameters:
  1134→    ///   - chunkId: The UUID of the chunk being transferred
  1135→    ///   - progress: Current progress (0.0 to 1.0)
  1136→    func updateChunkProgress(chunkId: UUID, progress: Double) {
  1137→        chunkTransferProgress[chunkId] = progress
  1138→        chunkLastProgressUpdate[chunkId] = Date()
  1139→    }
  1140→    
  1141→    /// Checks all in-flight transfers (chunked and single-file) for stalls.
  1142→    /// A transfer is stalled if progress hasn't changed past the timeout policy.
  1143→    private func checkForStalledTransfers() {
  1144→        guard transferState == .transferring || transferState == .retrying else { return }
  1145→        
  1146→        let now = Date()
  1147→        
  1148→        // Check retry timeout (15-minute window for retries)
  1149→        if transferState == .retrying, let retryStart = retryStartTime {
  1150→            if now.timeIntervalSince(retryStart) >= Self.retryTimeoutSeconds {
  1151→                RecordingLogger.error("Retry timeout reached (15 min) - triggering COMPLETE FAIL")
  1152→                handleCompleteFail()
  1153→                return
  1154→            }
  1155→        }
  1156→        
  1157→        // --- Check chunk-based transfers ---
  1158→        for (chunkId, lastUpdate) in chunkLastProgressUpdate {
  1159→            let progress = chunkTransferProgress[chunkId] ?? 0
  1160→            let timeSinceUpdate = now.timeIntervalSince(lastUpdate)
  1161→            let activeRecordingId = currentTransferRecordingId
  1162→            let timeout = transferTimeoutSeconds(for: activeRecordingId)
  1163→            
  1164→            if progress < 1.0 && timeSinceUpdate >= timeout {
  1165→                RecordingLogger.error("Chunk \(chunkId) stalled at \(Int(progress * 100))% for \(Int(timeSinceUpdate))s")
  1166→                handleTransferStalled(
  1167→                    recordingId: activeRecordingId,
  1168→                    stalledProgress: progress,
  1169→                    stalledDuration: timeSinceUpdate
  1170→                )
  1171→                return
  1172→            }
  1173→        }
  1174→        
  1175→        // --- Check single-file transfers ---
  1176→        // For non-chunked transfers, chunkLastProgressUpdate is empty so the loop
  1177→        // above is a no-op. Poll WatchConnectivityManager's per-recording progress.
  1178→        guard chunkLastProgressUpdate.isEmpty else { return }
  1179→        
  1180→        for recordingId in activeTransfers {
  1181→            let currentProgress = connectivityManager.recordingTransferProgress[recordingId] ?? 0
  1182→            let previousProgress = recordingLastProgressValue[recordingId] ?? -1
  1183→            let timeout = transferTimeoutSeconds(for: recordingId)
  1184→            
  1185→            if currentProgress != previousProgress {
  1186→                recordingLastProgressUpdate[recordingId] = now
  1187→                recordingLastProgressValue[recordingId] = currentProgress
  1188→            } else if let lastUpdate = recordingLastProgressUpdate[recordingId] {
  1189→                let timeSinceUpdate = now.timeIntervalSince(lastUpdate)
  1190→                if currentProgress < 1.0 && timeSinceUpdate >= timeout {
  1191→                    RecordingLogger.error("Single-file transfer \(recordingId) stalled at \(Int(currentProgress * 100))% for \(Int(timeSinceUpdate))s")
  1192→                    handleTransferStalled(
  1193→                        recordingId: recordingId,
  1194→                        stalledProgress: currentProgress,
  1195→                        stalledDuration: timeSinceUpdate
  1196→                    )
  1197→                    return
  1198→                }
  1199→            }

  1201→    }
  1202→    
  1203→    /// Handles a stalled transfer by cancelling the in-flight WCSession transfer
  1204→    /// and moving to failed state. The user must press RETRY to continue.
  1205→    private func handleTransferStalled(
  1206→        recordingId: UUID?,
  1207→        stalledProgress: Double,
  1208→        stalledDuration: TimeInterval
  1209→    ) {
  1210→        RecordingLogger.log("Transfer stalled — cancelling in-flight transfers before marking failed")
  1211→        
  1212→        // Cancel the existing WCSession transfer so the OS stops trying to deliver
  1213→        // the old payload. Without this, a retry would create a second parallel transfer.
  1214→        if let recordingId = recordingId ?? currentRecordingId {
  1215→            connectivityManager.cancelTransfer(recordingId: recordingId)
  1216→            RecordingLogger.log("Cancelled WCSession transfer for recording: \(recordingId)")
  1217→            timedOutTransferIds.insert(recordingId)
  1218→
  1219→            Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
  1220→                await self?.transferFailureNotificationService.notifyTransferFailed(recordingId: recordingId)
  1221→            }
  1222→        }
  1223→        
  1224→        let failedMessage = "Transfer stalled at \(Int(stalledProgress * 100))% for \(Int(stalledDuration))s. Retry will resend from scratch."
  1225→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
  1226→            self.transferState = .failed(failedMessage)
  1227→        }
  1228→        
  1229→        WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.failure)
  1230→    }
  1231→    
  1232→    /// Called when user presses RETRY from the failed state.
  1233→    /// Cancels any lingering WCSession transfers, then resends and starts a 15-minute timeout.
  1234→    func retryTransfer() {
  1235→        RecordingLogger.log("=== RETRY TRANSFER ===")
  1236→        
  1237→        guard case .failed = transferState else {
  1238→            RecordingLogger.log("Cannot retry - not in failed state (current: \(transferState))")
  1239→            return
  1240→        }
  1241→        
  1242→        // Cancel any lingering WCSession transfers to prevent duplicates
  1243→        if let recordingId = currentRecordingId {
  1244→            connectivityManager.cancelTransfer(recordingId: recordingId)
  1245→            RecordingLogger.log("Cancelled existing WCSession transfer before retry: \(recordingId)")
  1246→            if timedOutTransferIds.contains(recordingId) {
  1247→                // Timed-out transfers must retry from scratch to avoid partial-state divergence.
  1248→                requestCleanupPartialsOniPhone(recordingId: recordingId)
  1249→            }
  1250→        }
  1251→        
  1252→        transferState = .retrying
  1253→        retryStartTime = Date()
  1254→        
  1255→        // Retry pending transfers via the standard mechanism
  1256→        retryPendingTransfers()
  1257→        if let recordingId = currentRecordingId {
  1258→            timedOutTransferIds.remove(recordingId)
  1259→        }
  1260→        
  1261→        RecordingLogger.log("Retry started with 15-minute timeout")
  1262→        WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.start)
  1263→    }
  1264→
  1265→    private func transferTimeoutSeconds(for recordingId: UUID?) -> TimeInterval {
  1266→        guard let recordingId = recordingId,
  1267→              let recording = try? repository.fetchAllRecordings().first(where: { $0.id == recordingId }) else {
  1268→            return transferTimeoutPolicy.timeout(forRecordingDuration: nil)
  1269→        }
  1270→        return transferTimeoutPolicy.timeout(forRecordingDuration: recording.duration)
  1271→    }
  1272→
  1273→    private func requestCleanupPartialsOniPhone(recordingId: UUID) {
  1274→        connectivityManager.sendMessage(
  1275→            ["action": "cleanupPartials", "recordingId": recordingId.uuidString]
  1276→        ) { error in
  1277→            if let error = error {
  1278→                RecordingLogger.error("Failed to send cleanup message to iPhone before retry", error: error)
  1279→            }
  1280→        }
  1281→    }
  1282→    
  1283→    /// Handles complete failure: retry timed out after 15 minutes.
  1284→    /// Cancels all in-flight transfers, deletes partial data, then resends from scratch.
  1285→    private func handleCompleteFail() {
  1286→        RecordingLogger.error("=== COMPLETE FAIL - Deleting all partials and resending ===")
  1287→        
  1288→        transferState = .completeFail
  1289→        
  1290→        // Cancel ALL in-flight WCSession transfers for this recording first
  1291→        if let recordingId = currentRecordingId {
  1292→            connectivityManager.cancelTransfer(recordingId: recordingId)
  1293→            RecordingLogger.log("Cancelled all WCSession transfers for complete fail: \(recordingId)")
  1294→        }
  1295→        
  1296→        // Clear all chunk progress tracking
  1297→        chunkTransferProgress.removeAll()
  1298→        chunkLastProgressUpdate.removeAll()
  1299→        retryStartTime = nil
  1300→        
  1301→        // Notify iPhone to delete partial data via connectivity manager
  1302→        connectivityManager.sendMessage(
  1303→            ["action": "cleanupPartials", "recordingId": currentRecordingId?.uuidString ?? ""]
  1304→        ) { error in
  1305→            if let error = error {
  1306→                RecordingLogger.error("Failed to send cleanup message to iPhone", error: error)
  1307→            }
  1308→        }
  1309→        
  1310→        // Re-send all chunks from the beginning
  1311→        let chunkFiles = chunkedRecordingManager.getLocalChunkFiles()
  1312→        RecordingLogger.log("Resending \(chunkFiles.count) chunks from scratch")
  1313→        
  1314→        // Small delay to let cleanup propagate, then start fresh transfer
  1315→        DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 2.0) { [weak self] in
  1316→            guard let self = self else { return }
  1317→            self.transferState = .transferring
  1318→            self.retryStartTime = nil
  1319→            
  1320→            // Re-transfer all chunks
  1321→            self.retryPendingTransfers()
  1322→        }
  1323→        
  1324→        WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.failure)
  1325→    }
  1326→    
  1327→    func stopRecording() {
  1328→        RecordingLogger.log("=== STOP RECORDING ===")
  1329→        
  1330→        guard state == .recording || state == .paused else {
  1331→            RecordingLogger.log("Cannot stop - not in recording/paused state (current: \(state))")
  1332→            return
  1333→        }
  1334→        
  1335→        // ✅ Get the recording ID we created at start
  1336→        guard let recordingId = currentRecordingId else {
  1337→            RecordingLogger.error("No recording ID found - this should not happen!")
  1338→            state = .error("Internal error: no recording ID")
  1339→            WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.failure)
  1340→            return
  1341→        }
  1342→        
  1343→        state = .saving
  1344→        stopTimer()
  1345→        
  1346→        // Notify iPhone that recording stopped so it can end Live Activity
  1347→        WatchConnectivityManager.shared.sendRecordingOnWatchIndicator(isRecording: false, startedAt: nil)
  1348→        
  1349→        // Stop recording via ChunkedRecordingManager.
  1350→        // This finalizes the last chunk (marks isLastChunk = true).
  1351→        // No transfer happens yet — we send everything below after saving.
  1352→        let totalDuration = chunkedRecordingManager.stopRecording()
  1353→        
  1354→        RecordingLogger.log("Recording stopped. ID: \(recordingId), Total duration: \(totalDuration)s")
  1355→        
  1356→        // Save recording to local repository so it appears in Watch history.
  1357→        // Copy ALL chunks to the Recordings directory so that retries via transferToiOS
  1358→        // can find and re-send every chunk (not just the first).
  1359→        let chunkFiles = chunkedRecordingManager.getLocalChunkFiles()
  1360→        let sortedChunks = chunkFiles.sorted(by: { $0.key < $1.key })
  1361→        
  1362→        if let localRepo = repository as? LocalRecordingRepository {
  1363→            // Copy every chunk to the persistent Recordings directory
  1364→            for (chunkIndex, chunkURL) in sortedChunks {
  1365→                let destURL = localRepo.recordingsDirectoryURL
  1366→                    .appendingPathComponent(chunkURL.lastPathComponent)
  1367→                do {
  1368→                    if FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: destURL.path) {
  1369→                        try FileManager.default.removeItem(at: destURL)
  1370→                    }
  1371→                    try FileManager.default.copyItem(at: chunkURL, to: destURL)
  1372→                    RecordingLogger.log("  Persisted chunk \(chunkIndex) → \(destURL.lastPathComponent)")
  1373→                } catch {
  1374→                    RecordingLogger.error("Failed to persist chunk \(chunkIndex): \(error.localizedDescription)", error: error)
  1375→                }
  1376→            }
  1377→        }
  1378→        
  1379→        // Save first chunk as the representative file for the Core Data entry
  1380→        var savedRecordingForTransfer: Recording?
  1381→        if let firstChunkEntry = sortedChunks.first {
  1382→            do {
  1383→                let savedRecording = try repository.saveRecording(
  1384→                    id: recordingId,
  1385→                    fileURL: firstChunkEntry.1,
  1386→                    duration: totalDuration,
  1387→                    startTime: recordingStartTime
  1388→                )
  1389→                RecordingLogger.success("Saved recording to local repository: \(savedRecording.id), duration: \(totalDuration)s, totalChunks: \(sortedChunks.count)")
  1390→                lastRecording = savedRecording
  1391→                savedRecordingForTransfer = savedRecording
  1392→            } catch {
  1393→                RecordingLogger.error("Failed to save recording to local repository", error: error)
  1394→            }
  1395→        } else {
  1396→            RecordingLogger.error("No chunk files available to save recording to local repository")
  1397→        }
  1398→        
  1399→        // Queue-aware transfer start:
  1400→        // only one recording is sent at a time; pending recordings remain queued.
  1401→        if let recordingToTransfer = savedRecordingForTransfer {
  1402→            transferToiOS(recording: recordingToTransfer)
  1403→        } else {
  1404→            RecordingLogger.error("Skipping transfer start because recording could not be saved")
  1405→        }
  1406→        
  1407→        state = .idle
  1408→        accumulated = 0
  1409→        elapsed = 0
  1410→        remainingTime = 0
  1411→        isExtendEnabled = false
  1412→        activeAlert = nil
  1413→        shownAlerts.removeAll()
  1414→        WKInterfaceDevice.current().play(.success)
  1415→        
  1416→        // Stop keep-alive session - allow app to suspend normally now
  1417→        stopKeepAliveSession()
  1418→        
  1419→        // Cleanup recording state (keep currentRecordingId for stall detection reference)
  1420→        audioRecorder = nil
  1421→        currentRecordingURL = nil
  1422→        recordingStartTime = nil
  1423→        pauseReason = nil
  1424→        deactivateAudioSession()
  1425→        
  1426→        RecordingLogger.log("=== STOP RECORDING COMPLETE ===")
  1427→    }
  1428→    
  1429→    // MARK: - iOS Transfer
  1430→    
  1431→    /// Transfers a recording to the iOS app via WatchConnectivity.
  1432→    /// Chunk-aware: scans the Recordings directory for all chunk files belonging to
  1433→    /// this recording and sends them with proper `chunkInfo` metadata so the iPhone
  1434→    /// can assemble them. Falls back to a single-file transfer when only one file exists.
  1435→    /// - Parameter recording: The recording to transfer
  1436→    func transferToiOS(recording: Recording) {
  1437→        RecordingLogger.log("=== TRANSFER TO iOS ===")
  1438→        RecordingLogger.log("Recording ID: \(recording.id)")
  1439→        RecordingLogger.log("File URL: \(recording.fileURL.path)")
  1440→        RecordingLogger.log("Filename: \(recording.filename)")
  1441→        RecordingLogger.log("Duration: \(recording.duration)s")
  1442→        
  1443→        // Enforce one-recording-at-a-time transfer policy.
  1444→        if let activeId = currentTransferRecordingId, activeId != recording.id {
  1445→            RecordingLogger.log("⏳ Transfer slot busy with \(activeId). Keeping \(recording.id) queued.")
  1446→            return
  1447→        }
  1448→        if let activeId = activeTransfers.first, activeId != recording.id {
  1449→            currentTransferRecordingId = activeId
  1450→            RecordingLogger.log("⏳ Transfer already active for \(activeId). Keeping \(recording.id) queued.")
  1451→            return
  1452→        }
  1453→        
  1454→        // Check if this recording is already being transferred
  1455→        if connectivityManager.isRecordingTransferring(recording.id) {
  1456→            RecordingLogger.log("⚠️ Recording \(recording.id) is already being transferred - skipping duplicate transfer")
  1457→            return
  1458→        }
  1459→        
  1460→        // Check if recording can be sent using Core Data status
  1461→        if let localRepo = repository as? LocalRecordingRepository {
  1462→            let (canSend, reason) = localRepo.canSendRecording(recording.id)
  1463→            if !canSend {
  1464→                RecordingLogger.log("⚠️ Cannot send recording: \(reason ?? "unknown")")
  1465→                return
  1466→            }
  1467→        }
  1468→        
  1469→        // Check if companion app is installed
  1470→        guard connectivityManager.isWatchConnected else {
  1471→            RecordingLogger.log("iOS app not connected - recording will stay local until sync")
  1472→            return
  1473→        }
  1474→        
  1475→        // Discover all chunk files for this recording in the Recordings directory.
  1476→        // Files follow the naming convention: {recordingID}_chunk{index}.m4a
  1477→        let chunkFiles = discoverChunkFiles(for: recording)
  1478→        
  1479→        guard !chunkFiles.isEmpty else {
  1480→            RecordingLogger.error("Cannot transfer - no chunk files found for recording: \(recording.id)")
  1481→            return
  1482→        }
  1483→        
  1484→        // Update status to transferring in Core Data
  1485→        if let localRepo = repository as? LocalRecordingRepository {
  1486→            do {
  1487→                try localRepo.updateStatus(recording.id, status: .transferring)
  1488→                RecordingLogger.log("✅ Updated status to transferring")
  1489→            } catch {
  1490→                RecordingLogger.error("Failed to update status", error: error)
  1491→            }
  1492→        }
  1493→        
  1494→        // Update transfer state
  1495→        currentTransferRecordingId = recording.id
  1496→        transferState = .transferring
  1497→        isTransferringToiOS = true
  1498→        activeTransfers.insert(recording.id)
  1499→

1→import Foundation
     2→import WatchKit
     3→
     4→// MARK: - Extended Runtime Session Manager
     5→
     6→/// Manages WKExtendedRuntimeSession to keep the watch app running during recording
     7→/// even when the wrist is down or the screen is off.
     8→/// 
     9→/// This manager handles the lifecycle of extended runtime sessions, which are required
    10→/// for continuous background execution on watchOS (e.g., during audio recording).
    11→///
    12→/// - Note: Extended runtime sessions have time limits (typically ~4 hours on watchOS 9+)
    13→///   and will drain battery faster than normal app execution.
    14→final class ExtendedRuntimeSessionManager: NSObject {
    15→    
    16→    // MARK: - Types
    17→    
    18→    /// Callback type for session state changes
    19→    typealias SessionStateCallback = (SessionState) -> Void
    20→    
    21→    /// Callback type for session expiration warnings
    22→    typealias ExpirationCallback = () -> Void
    23→    
    24→    /// Callback type for session invalidation
    25→    typealias InvalidationCallback = (WKExtendedRuntimeSessionInvalidationReason, Error?) -> Void
    26→    
    27→    /// Represents the current state of the extended runtime session
    28→    enum SessionState {
    29→        case idle           // No active session
    30→        case starting       // Session is being started
    31→        case active         // Session is active and running
    32→        case expiring       // Session is about to expire
    33→        case invalidated    // Session has been invalidated
    34→    }
    35→    
    36→    // MARK: - Properties
    37→    
    38→    /// The current extended runtime session (nil when not active)
    39→    private var session: WKExtendedRuntimeSession?
    40→    
    41→    /// Holds a strong reference to the old session while its async invalidation
    42→    /// completes. Prevents dealloc-while-running crashes.
    43→    private var previousSession: WKExtendedRuntimeSession?
    44→    
    45→    /// Current state of the session
    46→    private(set) var state: SessionState = .idle {
    47→        didSet {
    48→            stateCallback?(state)
    49→        }
    50→    }
    51→    
    52→    /// Callback invoked when session state changes
    53→    private var stateCallback: SessionStateCallback?
    54→    
    55→    /// Callback invoked when session is about to expire
    56→    private var expirationCallback: ExpirationCallback?
    57→    
    58→    /// Callback invoked when session is invalidated
    59→    private var invalidationCallback: InvalidationCallback?
    60→    
    61→    /// Logger for session operations
    62→    private let logger = LoggerFactory.makeLogger()
    63→    
    64→    /// Convenience logging methods
    65→    private func log(_ message: String, function: String = #function) {
    66→        logger.info("🔄 [ExtendedSession] [\(function)] \(message)")
    67→    }
    68→    
    69→    private func logError(_ message: String, error: Error? = nil, function: String = #function) {
    70→        logger.error("❌ [ExtendedSession] [\(function)] \(message)", error: error)
    71→    }
    72→    
    73→    private func logSuccess(_ message: String, function: String = #function) {
    74→        logger.info("✅ [ExtendedSession] [\(function)] \(message)")
    75→    }
    76→    
    77→    // MARK: - Initialization
    78→    
    79→    override init() {
    80→        super.init()
    81→        log("ExtendedRuntimeSessionManager initialized")
    82→    }
    83→    
    84→    deinit {
    85→        if let session = session,
    86→           session.state == .running || session.state == .scheduled {
    87→            session.invalidate()
    88→        }
    89→        session = nil
    90→        previousSession = nil
    91→    }
    92→    
    93→    // MARK: - Public Methods
    94→    
    95→    /// Starts an extended runtime session to keep the app running in the background
    96→    /// - Parameters:
    97→    ///   - onStateChange: Optional callback invoked when session state changes
    98→    ///   - onExpiration: Optional callback invoked when session is about to expire
    99→    ///   - onInvalidation: Optional callback invoked when session is invalidated
   100→    /// - Returns: True if session start was initiated, false if already active or failed
   101→    func startSession(
   102→        onStateChange: SessionStateCallback? = nil,
   103→        onExpiration: ExpirationCallback? = nil,
   104→        onInvalidation: InvalidationCallback? = nil
   105→    ) -> Bool {
   106→        log("=== START EXTENDED RUNTIME SESSION ===")
   107→        
   108→        // If there's an existing session, move it to previousSession so it stays
   109→        // alive until the system finishes invalidating it asynchronously.
   110→        if let oldSession = session {
   111→            log("Existing session found — parking it while starting a new one")
   112→            previousSession = oldSession
   113→            if oldSession.state == .running || oldSession.state == .scheduled {
   114→                oldSession.invalidate()
   115→            }
   116→            session = nil
   117→        }
   118→        
   119→        // Store callbacks
   120→        self.stateCallback = onStateChange
   121→        self.expirationCallback = onExpiration
   122→        self.invalidationCallback = onInvalidation
   123→        
   124→        // Create and configure new session
   125→        let newSession = WKExtendedRuntimeSession()
   126→        newSession.delegate = self
   127→        self.session = newSession
   128→        
   129→        // Update state to starting
   130→        state = .starting
   131→        
   132→        newSession.start()
   133→        
   134→        logSuccess("Extended runtime session start initiated")
   135→        return true
   136→    }
   137→    
   138→    /// Stops and invalidates the current extended runtime session
   139→    /// This should be called when recording stops to allow the app to suspend normally
   140→    func stopSession() {
   141→        log("=== STOP EXTENDED RUNTIME SESSION ===")
   142→        
   143→        guard session != nil else {
   144→            log("No active session to stop")
   145→            return
   146→        }
   147→        
   148→        invalidateSession()
   149→        logSuccess("Extended runtime session stopped")
   150→    }
   151→    
   152→    /// Checks if there is an active extended runtime session
   153→    /// - Returns: True if session is active, false otherwise
   154→    var isActive: Bool {
   155→        return state == .active || state == .expiring
   156→    }
   157→    
   158→    // MARK: - Private Methods
   159→    
   160→    /// Invalidates the current session.
   161→    /// Does NOT nil the session reference — the delegate callback handles cleanup
   162→    /// so the object stays alive until the system finishes invalidation.
   163→    private func invalidateSession() {
   164→        guard let session = session else {
   165→            return
   166→        }
   167→        
   168→        log("Invalidating extended runtime session (state: \(session.state.rawValue))")
   169→        
   170→        if session.state == .running || session.state == .scheduled {
   171→            session.invalidate()
   172→        } else {
   173→            log("Session not running (state: \(session.state.rawValue)) — cleaning up reference")
   174→            // Session is already not running, safe to clear immediately
   175→            self.session = nil
   176→            stateCallback = nil
   177→            expirationCallback = nil
   178→            invalidationCallback = nil
   179→            state = .invalidated
   180→        }
   181→    }
   182→}
   183→
   184→// MARK: - WKExtendedRuntimeSessionDelegate
   185→
   186→extension ExtendedRuntimeSessionManager: WKExtendedRuntimeSessionDelegate {
   187→    
   188→    /// Called when the extended runtime session successfully starts
   189→    /// - Parameter extendedRuntimeSession: The session that started
   190→    func extendedRuntimeSessionDidStart(_ extendedRuntimeSession: WKExtendedRuntimeSession) {
   191→        logSuccess("Extended runtime session started successfully")
   192→        log("Session will keep app running even when wrist is down")
   193→        
   194→        DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   195→            self?.state = .active
   196→        }
   197→    }
   198→    
   199→    /// Called when the extended runtime session is about to expire
   200→    /// This typically happens when approaching time limits (e.g., ~4 hours)
   201→    /// - Parameter extendedRuntimeSession: The session that is expiring
   202→    func extendedRuntimeSessionWillExpire(_ extendedRuntimeSession: WKExtendedRuntimeSession) {
   203→        logger.warning("⚠️ [ExtendedSession] Extended runtime session is about to expire - Recording may be interrupted soon")
   204→        
   205→        DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   206→            guard let self = self else { return }
   207→            
   208→            // Update state to expiring
   209→            self.state = .expiring
   210→            
   211→            // Notify via callback
   212→            self.expirationCallback?()
   213→        }
   214→    }
   215→    
   216→    /// Called when the extended runtime session is invalidated
   217→    /// This can happen due to expiration, system termination, or manual invalidation
   218→    /// - Parameters:
   219→    ///   - extendedRuntimeSession: The session that was invalidated
   220→    ///   - reason: The reason for invalidation
   221→    ///   - error: Optional error if invalidation was due to an error
   222→    func extendedRuntimeSession(
   223→        _ extendedRuntimeSession: WKExtendedRuntimeSession,
   224→        didInvalidateWith reason: WKExtendedRuntimeSessionInvalidationReason,
   225→        error: Error?
   226→    ) {
   227→        let reasonDescription: String
   228→        switch reason {
   229→        case .none:
   230→            reasonDescription = AppText.SessionInvalidation.none
   231→        case .sessionInProgress:
   232→            reasonDescription = AppText.SessionInvalidation.sessionInProgress
   233→        case .expired:
   234→            reasonDescription = AppText.SessionInvalidation.expired
   235→        case .resignedFrontmost:
   236→            reasonDescription = AppText.SessionInvalidation.resignedFrontmost
   237→        case .suppressedBySystem:
   238→            reasonDescription = AppText.SessionInvalidation.suppressedBySystem
   239→        case .error:
   240→            reasonDescription = AppText.SessionInvalidation.error
   241→        @unknown default:
   242→            reasonDescription = AppText.SessionInvalidation.unknown(reason.rawValue)
   243→        }
   244→        
   245→        if let error = error {
   246→            logError("Extended runtime session invalidated - Reason: \(reasonDescription)", error: error)
   247→        } else {
   248→            logger.warning("⚠️ [ExtendedSession] Extended runtime session invalidated - Reason: \(reasonDescription)")
   249→        }
   250→        
   251→        DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   252→            guard let self = self else { return }
   253→            
   254→            // If this is the old "parked" session finishing invalidation, just release it
   255→            if extendedRuntimeSession === self.previousSession {
   256→                self.log("Previous session finished invalidating — releasing reference")
   257→                self.previousSession = nil
   258→                return
   259→            }
   260→            
   261→            // This is the current session — full cleanup
   262→            self.invalidationCallback?(reason, error)
   263→            
   264→            self.session = nil
   265→            self.state = .invalidated
   266→            
   267→            self.stateCallback = nil
   268→            self.expirationCallback = nil
   269→            self.invalidationCallback = nil
   270→        }
   271→    }
   272→}
   273→
   274→
